Ôªø# APIkit - Full Documentation

# Display a Typed SOAP Fault

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Configure the `ListInventory` operation in APIkit for SOAP so responses surface the **TshirtFault**
typed fault from `tshirt2.wsdl`, which also maps faults to `OrderTshirt`, `ListInventory`, and
`TrackOrder`. Select Soap Fault 1.1 or Soap Fault 1.2 for any WSDL, including services that do not
declare typed faults.

1.  In Anypoint Studio, search for **fault** in the command palette.

    Multiple `SOAP Fault` components can appear.

2.  Drag `SOAP Fault` to the `ListInventory:api-config` flow.

    If **Operation Unavailable** message appears, cancel the operation and select another
    `SOAP Fault` component.

3.  Repeat these steps until you find a version match.
4.  In the properties editor, in **Module Configuration**, select `soapkit-config`.
5.  In **Operation**, select `ListInventory`.
6.  In **Fault**, select `Fault`.
7.  Save the properties.
8.  Select the `Transform Message` component.
9.  Modify the DataWeave code as follows:

    ```raml
    %dw 2.0
    output application/xml
    ns ns0 http://mulesoft.org/tshirt-service
    ns soap http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/
    ---
    {
      soap#Fault: {
        faultcode: "soap:Server",
        faultstring: "The error details",
        detail: {
          ns0#TshirtFault: {
            errorStuff: 500
          }
        }
      }
    }
    ```

10. Save the project and run it again.

    Using SoapUI, run the `ListInventory` operation.

    The client returns the following envelope instead of the default fault:

    ```xml
    <soap:Envelope xmlns:soap="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/">
       <soap:Body>
          <soap:Fault>
             <faultcode>soap:Server</faultcode>
             <faultstring>The error details</faultstring>
             <detail>
                <ns0:TshirtFault xmlns:ns0="http://mulesoft.org/tshirt-service">
                   <errorStuff>500</errorStuff>
                </ns0:TshirtFault>
             </detail>
          </soap:Fault>
       </soap:Body>
    </soap:Envelope>
    ```

## See Also

- [Get a SOAP Header](apikit-4-get-header-task)
---

# APIkit for GraphQL

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

APIkit for GraphQL implements GraphQL services in Mule applications and integrates with Anypoint
Code Builder so you connect schemas to backends through connectors. The APIkit scaffolder generates
the application skeleton from your GraphQL schema at design time and maps queries, mutations, and
types to flows. At run time, the GraphQL router walks the query graph, runs the mapped flows, and
assembles a response that matches the requested query shape.

## See Also

- [Implement a GraphQL API](../../anypoint-code-builder/tut-graphql-implement-api)
- [Mapping a GraphQL API to Your Data Sources](apikit-graphql-api-mapping)
- [Querying a GraphQL API Implementation](apikit-graphql-api-implementation)
- [APIkit for GraphQL Module Reference](apikit-graphql-module-reference)
---

# APIkit for OData V2

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

APIkit for OData v2 uses standard HTTP to build RESTful APIs that follow the OData v2 specification
and the Open Data Protocol (OData) for querying and updating data. The extension layers on APIkit
for REST and RAML contracts so you combine OData v2 tooling with the same REST design workflow. It
offers fewer capabilities than OData v4 and some newer stacks; choose OData v4 when you need the
latest protocol features and query model.

## See Also

- [APIkit for OData V4](apikit-4-for-odatav4)
- [Installing OData V2 Tooling](install-odatav2-tooling)
---

# APIkit for OData V4

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

APIkit for OData v4 delivers RESTful APIs that comply with the OData v4 specification. It uses
standard HTTP for querying and updating data through the Open Data Protocol (OData). Generate Mule
flows that handle OData requests so you can modernize legacy APIs, orchestrate data into Salesforce,
or expose bidirectional OData surfaces for databases such as Oracle, DB2, or Azure DB. Unlike the
RAML-based APIkit for OData v2 extension, APIkit for OData v4 uses the OData Entity Data Model (EDM)
to describe resources and structures for HTTP-based data services.

## See Also

- [APIkit for OData V2](apikit-4-for-odatav2)
- [Installing OData V4 Tooling](install-odatav4-tooling)
---

# APIkit for REST

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

APIkit for REST streamlines development and integration of RESTful APIs with RAML or OAS contracts,
validation, generated implementation templates, and routing that follows your API design.
Representational State Transfer (REST) defines how web services expose resources; APIkit for REST
turns those contracts into consistent Mule flows. Define RAML or OpenAPI Specification (OAS) up
front to align design, documentation, and runtime behavior across your team.

## Features

- Automatic Validation

  APIkit for REST automatically validates incoming requests against your API contracts defined in
  RAML or OAS. This validation ensures that requests adhere to the specified rules and constraints
  of the API.

- Code Generation

  The framework provides code generation capabilities that help streamline the implementation of
  your RESTful APIs. APIkit for REST generates implementation templates based on your API contracts.

- Intelligent Routing

  With APIkit for REST, incoming requests are intelligently routed to the appropriate endpoints
  based on the defined API contracts.

## See Also

- [Building, Implementing, and Testing a REST API](apikit-4-implement-rest-api)
- [APIkit for REST Module Reference](apikit-4-xml-reference)
---

# APIkit for SOAP

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

APIkit for SOAP simplifies building and integrating SOAP-based web services by handling message
formats, headers, and WSDL binding against your existing SOAP endpoints. SOAP (Simple Object Access
Protocol) exchanges structured payloads between services. APIkit for SOAP keeps those payloads
aligned with your contracts. To learn more about automated configurations, data transformations, XML
1.0 defaults, and optional XML 1.1 C0 characters, see
[system property](apikit-for-soap-module-reference#apikit-for-soap-system-properties).

## Choose the APIkit for SOAP Module Version

APIkit for SOAP now has two versions so you can align the module with your Mule runtime and JDK
versions. Choose the version that matches the platform you deploy to today. For more information
about installation, see [Before You Begin Using APIkit for SOAP](apikit-4-soap-prerequisites-task).

> [!NOTE] APIkit for SOAP 1.6.5 through 1.6.10 are deprecated. Move to version 1.7.0 (legacy
> compatibility) or 2.0.0 (modern major).

### APIkit for SOAP Versions

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Line</th><th>Version</th><th>Minimum Platform</th><th>Use Cases</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Legacy compatibility</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>1.7.0</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>JDK 8, 11, or 17; Mule runtime 4.1.x and later</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>You must keep existing deployments on earlier Mule or Java stacks without adopting the 2.x requirements.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Modern major</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>2.0.0 (successor to 1.6.10)</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>JDK 17 only; Mule runtime 4.9.11 and later</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>You standardize on current Mule and Java 17. You choose the version that receives new features.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Deprecated</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>1.6.5 √¢¬Ä¬ì 1.6.10</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Don√¢¬Ä¬ôt adopt or standardize on these releases.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Migrate to 1.7.0 or 2.0.0 using the <a href="https://docs.mulesoft.com/apikit/latest/apikit-4-for-soap#choose-the-apikit-for-soap-module-version">version selection guide</a>.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## APIkit for SOAP Features

- Streamlines SOAP API development, ensuring seamless integration with your existing SOAP
  infrastructure.
- Simplifies the handling of SOAP message formats and headers, so you can focus on the core logic of
  your APIs.
- Automates the binding to WSDL (Web Services Description Language), reducing manual configuration
  efforts and ensuring accuracy.
- Supports data transformations.
- Defines and exposes SOAP APIs with ease by emphasizing SOAP/WSDL contracts.
- Supports XML 1.0 characters by default. Supports XML 1.1 C0 characters through the
  [system property](apikit-for-soap-module-reference#apikit-for-soap-system-properties).

## See Also

- [Before You Begin Using APIkit for SOAP](apikit-4-soap-prerequisites-task)
---

# Get a SOAP Header

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Retrieve a SOAP header value from an incoming request when you exercise APIs with SoapUI and
Anypoint Studio. This walkthrough reads the API key from the `OrderTshirt` envelope and surfaces it
through DataWeave in the `OrderTshirt:\soapkit-config` flow. You update Transform Message, rerun the
app, and confirm the header appears in the response.

![Request window showing the SOAP envelope header](_images/apikit-for-soap-53dc2.png)

1.  In SoapUI, modify the `OrderTshirt` request by entering a value for the API key.

    For example, enter `987654321` between the `<apiKey>` tags.

2.  In Studio, in the `OrderTshirt:\soapkit-config` flow, select **Transform Message**.
3.  Edit the DataWeave code to get the API key, which is a header property:

    ```raml
    %dw 2.0
    output application/java
    ns ns0 http://mulesoft.org/tshirt-service
    ---
    {
          body : {
             ns0#OrderTshirtResponse: {
               orderId: "I got a request from "
               ++ payload.body.ns0#OrderTshirt.name
               ++ " using the following auth header "
               ++ (payload.headers["AuthenticationHeader"].ns0#AuthenticationHeader.apiKey default "")
            }
        } write "application/xml"
    }
    ```

4.  Save and run the project again.
5.  In SoapUI, send a request to the `OrderTshirt` endpoint. + The following response appears:

    ```xml
       <soap:Body>
    <soap:Envelope xmlns:soap="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/">
          <ns0:OrderTshirtResponse xmlns:ns0="http://mulesoft.org/tshirt-service">
             <orderId>I got a request from John, using the following auth header 987654321</orderId>
          </ns0:OrderTshirtResponse>
       </soap:Body>
    </soap:Envelope>
    ```

## See Also

- [Set a SOAP Header](apikit-4-set-header-task)
---

# Building, Implementing, and Testing a REST API

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Follow this end-to-end workflow to design, implement, and test a REST API in Anypoint Studio. The
example relies on the public [{JSON} Placeholder](https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com) service,
which returns mocked user JSON so you can build and validate flows without hosting a backend.

## Before You Begin

Ensure you have the following software installed:

- Anypoint Studio 7.1.4 or later
- Mule runtime engine 4.1.1 or later

## Design Your API Specification

Design your API specification with API Designer from Design Center, with the API Specification
Project tool from Anypoint Studio, or with a text editor of your choice:

1.  Create a JSON file including a list of users that serve as an example of what the service
    returns:

    \[ { "id": 1, "name": "Leanne Graham", "username": "Bret", "email": "Sincere@april.biz",
    "address": { "street": "Kulas Light", "suite": "Apt. 556", "city": "Gwenborough", "zipcode":
    "92998-3874", "geo": { "lat": "-37.3159", "lng": "81.1496" } }, "phone": "1-770-736-8031
    x56442", "website": "hildegard.org", "company": { "name": "Romaguera-Crona", "catchPhrase":
    "Multi-layered client-server neural-net", "bs": "harness real-time e-markets" } } \]

2.  Save it as `user-example.json`.
3.  Create a new blank file with the following API specification:

    ```yaml
    #%RAML 1.0
    title: placeholder
    version: 1.0
    /users:
      get:
        description: Retrieve a list of all the users
        responses:
          200:
            body:
              application/json:
                example: !include user-example.json
      /userbyid:
        get:
          description: Get information about a particular user
          queryParameters:
            id:
              description: Specify the id of the user you want to retrieve
              type: integer
              required: false
              example: 3
          responses:
            200:
              body:
                application/json:
                  example: |
                    [
                      {
                        "id": 3,
                        "name": "Clementine Bauch",
                        "username": "Samantha",
                        "email": "Nathan@yesenia.net",
                        "address":
                          {
                            "street": "Douglas Extension",
                            "suite": "Suite 847",
                            "city": "McKenziehaven",
                            "zipcode": "59590-4157",
                            "geo": { "lat": "-68.6102", "lng": "-47.0653" },
                          },
                        "phone": "1-463-123-4447",
                        "website": "ramiro.info",
                        "company":
                          {
                            "name": "Romaguera-Jacobson",
                            "catchPhrase": "Face to face bifurcated interface",
                            "bs": "e-enable strategic applications",
                          }
                      }
                    ]
    ```

4.  Save it as `api.raml`.
5.  In Studio, select **File** > **New** > **Mule Project**.
6.  In **Project Name**, type the name of your Mule project.
    - If you are importing your API specification from Exchange, select **Import a published API**,
      click the plus icon, and select **From Exchange**.

      ![*Import a published API* highlighted when clicking on plus to select the From Exchange option](_images/import-from-exchange.png)

    - If you are importing your API specification from a local file in your file system, select
      **Import RAML from Local File**.

      ![*Import RAML from local file* highlighted in the API implementation section](_images/import-from-local-file.png)

    - If you are importing your API specification from Design Center, select **Download RAML from
      Design Center**.

      ![*Import from Design Center* highlighted in the API implementation section](_images/import-from-design-center.png)

7.  Select **Scaffold flows from these API specifications** if not already selected.
8.  Click **Finish**.

    APIkit generates the necessary flows based on the API specification you imported.

9.  Run your APIkit project by clicking **Run** > **Run As** > **Mule Application**.

## Simulate API Calls

1.  In Anypoint Studio, locate the **APIkit Consoles** view and click **Open console** under the
    base point URL `http://localhost:8081/console/`.

    ![APIkit console tab displays the location of the basepoint URL in the window.](_images/apikit-consoles.png)

2.  Your default browser opens. The **API Console** shows the API summary and information about the
    project, such as the title, version, and supported protocols.

    ![API console version info](_images/apikit-console1.png)

3.  In **API Summary**, select `GET` for the `/users` resource.

    It opens a detail page of the resource showing, among others, the expected JSON example for an
    HTTP 200 response.

    ![APIkit response](_images/apikit-console3.png)

4.  In **API Summary**, expand `/userbyid`.
5.  Select `GET` for the `/usersbyid` resource.
6.  Click `Try it` to expand the view for sending a request.

    The **API Console** displays a section to add parameters and headers to try a request.

7.  Select **Show optional parameters** if not selected.
8.  Type `3` as a query parameter value for the **id** parameter and click `Send`.

    An HTTP 200 response confirms the successful request for the `/usersbyid` resource, which
    contains user information for the user with ID 3. This response also includes the body with a
    JSON example.

    ![Information for the user having id 3 highlighted](_images/apikit-console4.png)

## Add Business Logic to Your API Implementation

Modify the flows to return actual data from the JSON Placeholder service instead of the examples
from the API.

1.  Drag a new **Flow** component from the **Mule Palette**.
2.  Rename the new flow as `user_request_flow`.
3.  Drag an **HTTP Request** component to the flow process.
4.  In the properties editor of **Request**, in **General**, set the following HTTP request options:
    - In **Method**, leave the default `GET`.
    - In **Path**, set to `/users`.
    - On **Query Parameters**, select **Switch to expression mode** and paste the following
      DataWeave script:

      ## output application/java

      if (not isEmpty(attributes.queryParams)) { id : attributes.queryParams.id } else {}

5.  Delete **Transform Message** component in `get:\users:api-config` and
    `get:\users\userbyid:api-config` flows.
6.  Drag one **Flow Reference** for each flow in replacement of the removed **Transform Message**
    components.
7.  In the properties editor of **Flow Reference** components, set flow name as `user_request_flow`.
8.  Save changes.

## Test Your API Implementation

1.  In Package Explorer, right-click the project name and choose **Run As** > **Mule Application**.
2.  Open a browser and go to `http://localhost:8081/api/users`.

    The user information from the JSON Placeholder service is returned:

    \[ { "id": 1, "name": "Leanne Graham", "username": "Bret", "email": "Sincere@april.biz",
    "address": { "street": "Kulas Light", "suite": "Apt. 556", "city": "Gwenborough", "zipcode":
    "92998-3874", "geo": { "lat": "-37.3159", "lng": "81.1496"

        ...
        {
        "id": 10,
        "name": "Clementina DuBuque",
        "username": "Moriah.Stanton",
        "email": "Rey.Padberg@karina.biz",
        "address": {
          "street": "Kattie Turnpike",
          "suite": "Suite 198",
          "city": "Lebsackbury",
          "zipcode": "31428-2261",
          "geo": {
            "lat": "-38.2386",
            "lng": "57.2232"
          }

    ... \]

3.  Get information about the user having ID = 4 by going to
    `http://localhost:8081/api/users/userbyid?id=4`.

    \[ { "id": 4, "name": "Patricia Lebsack", "username": "Karianne", "email":
    "Julianne.OConner@kory.org", "address": { "street": "Hoeger Mall", "suite": "Apt. 692", "city":
    "South Elvis", "zipcode": "53919-4257", "geo": { "lat": "29.4572", "lng": "-164.2990" } },
    "phone": "493-170-9623 x156", "website": "kale.biz", "company": { "name": "Robel-Corkery",
    "catchPhrase": "Multi-tiered zero tolerance productivity", "bs": "transition cutting-edge web
    services" } } \]

Your REST API creation, implementation, and testing are complete.

## See Also

- [{JSON} Placeholder](http://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com)
- [Tutorial: Build an API from Start to Finish](../../general/api-led-overview)
- [Create and Publish an API Specification or API Fragment with Design Center](../../design-center/design-create-publish-api-specs)
---

# Return a Valid SOAP Response After a Fault

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

After you resolve the [SOAP fault](apikit-4-soap-fault-task), add Transform Message on the
`OrderTshirt:\soapkit-config` flow and author DataWeave that returns a valid payload instead of
another fault. Use the correct `namespace` so the response matches the WSDL contract and avoids
leading fault codes. Transform Message runs after the WSDL endpoint dispatches into your main flow
and any backend calls.

1.  In Studio, in the **OrderTshirt:\\soapkit-config** flow, open the Transform Message component.

    The Transform Message properties window displays the generated DataWeave code.

2.  Modify the DataWeave code as follows:

    ```raml
    %dw 2.0
    output application/java
    ns ns0 http://mulesoft.org/tshirt-service
    ---
    {
        body: {
            ns0#OrderTshirtResponse: {
                orderId: "I got a request from " ++ (payload.body.ns0#OrderTshirt.name default "")
            }
        } write "application/xml"
    }
    ```

3.  Save and rerun the project.
4.  In SoapUI, resubmit the request to generate a SOAP fault.

    The output changes to a valid response:

```xml
<soap:Envelope xmlns:soap="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/">
   <soap:Body>
      <ns0:OrderTshirtResponse xmlns:ns0="http://mulesoft.org/tshirt-service">
         <orderId>I got a request from John</orderId>
      </ns0:OrderTshirtResponse>
   </soap:Body>
</soap:Envelope>
```

## See Also

- [Display a Typed SOAP Fault](apikit-4-display-fault-task)
---

# APIkit for REST Scaffolding Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

APIkit for REST scaffolders generate Mule flows and supporting components from RESTful API Modeling
Language (RAML) or OpenAPI Specification (OAS) contracts. Use Anypoint Studio to import an API from
Anypoint Exchange, Design Center, or local files and produce the implementation backbone
automatically. Scaffolding wires the HTTP listener, APIkit Router, API console flow, and per-action
flows you replace with business logic.

## API Scaffolding

Studio offers several ways to scaffold an API:

- From **New Mule Project wizard**, select **Scaffold Flows From These API Specifications** when
  importing a published API from Exchange or Maven, importing from a local file, or downloading from
  Design Center.
- From **Package Explorer**, right-click the API file and select **Mule** > **Generate flows from
  Local REST API**.
- From **Package Explorer**, right-click the API dependency and select **Generate flows** from its
  context menu.

> [!NOTE] Scaffolding from a local file is not supported for OAS. Scaffolding OAS APIs is available
> only for published assets in Exchange.

## Implementation Reference

When scaffolding a REST API specification, APIkit generates the following components:

- A main flow that acts as the entry point and includes:
  - HTTP Listener as a source
  - APIkit Router as the main operation

    APIkit Router is a message processor that directs messages from incoming API requests to the
    corresponding Mule flows and generates a serialized response using default values. For example,
    in an invalid request, APIkit generates a `bad request` response, and a missing associated flow
    for the requested API resource results in a `not implemented` response.

    While APIkit Router validates aspects such as request payload, headers, query parameters, and
    URI parameters, it does not validate all elements specified in an API specification.
    Specifically, the router does not perform security-related validation, such as authentication
    enforcement. Instead, the API gateway policies are responsible for this validation, which occurs
    before the request reaches the router.

  - Error Handler

- A secondary flow that exposes the API console and includes:
  - HTTP Listener as a source
  - APIkit Console as the main operation
  - Error Handler

- In addition to N flows, one per API action-resource-content-type combination, each flow includes:
  - No source (this option is available for APIkit for REST and SOAP applications only)
  - A mock implementation you substitute later with your actual business logic

## Supported MIME Types

APIkit for REST supports the following MIME types:

- JSON (application/json)
- XML (application/xml)
- Multipart Form Data (multipart/form-data)
- URL Encoded Form Data (application/x-www-form-urlencoded)

## Example

```xml
<mule>
    <apikit:config name="api-config" api="api.raml" outboundHeadersMapName="outboundHeaders" httpStatusVarName="httpStatus" />
    <flow name="api-main">
        <http:listener config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/api/*">
            <http:response statusCode="#[vars.httpStatus default 200]">
                <http:headers>#[vars.outboundHeaders default {}]</http:headers>
            </http:response>
            <http:error-response statusCode="#[vars.httpStatus default 500]">
                <http:body>#[payload]</http:body>
                <http:headers>#[vars.outboundHeaders default {}]</http:headers>
            </http:error-response>
        </http:listener>
        <apikit:router config-ref="api-config" />
        <error-handler>
            <on-error-propagate type="APIKIT:BAD_REQUEST">
                <ee:transform>
                    <ee:message>
                        <ee:set-payload><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/json
---
{message: "Bad request"}]]></ee:set-payload>
                    </ee:message>
                    <ee:variables>
                        <ee:set-variable variableName="httpStatus">400</ee:set-variable>
                    </ee:variables>
                </ee:transform>
            </on-error-propagate>
            <on-error-propagate type="APIKIT:NOT_FOUND">
                <ee:transform>
                    <ee:message>
                        <ee:set-payload><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/json
---
{message: "Resource not found"}]]></ee:set-payload>
                    </ee:message>
                    <ee:variables>
                        <ee:set-variable variableName="httpStatus">404</ee:set-variable>
                    </ee:variables>
                </ee:transform>
            </on-error-propagate>
            <on-error-propagate type="APIKIT:METHOD_NOT_ALLOWED">
                <ee:transform>
                    <ee:message>
                        <ee:set-payload><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/json
---
{message: "Method not allowed"}]]></ee:set-payload>
                    </ee:message>
                    <ee:variables>
                        <ee:set-variable variableName="httpStatus">405</ee:set-variable>
                    </ee:variables>
                </ee:transform>
            </on-error-propagate>
            <on-error-propagate type="APIKIT:NOT_ACCEPTABLE">
                <ee:transform>
                    <ee:message>
                        <ee:set-payload><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/json
---
{message: "Not acceptable"}]]></ee:set-payload>
                    </ee:message>
                    <ee:variables>
                        <ee:set-variable variableName="httpStatus">406</ee:set-variable>
                    </ee:variables>
                </ee:transform>
            </on-error-propagate>
            <on-error-propagate type="APIKIT:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE">
                <ee:transform>
                    <ee:message>
                        <ee:set-payload><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/json
---
{message: "Unsupported media type"}]]></ee:set-payload>
                    </ee:message>
                    <ee:variables>
                        <ee:set-variable variableName="httpStatus">415</ee:set-variable>
                    </ee:variables>
                </ee:transform>
            </on-error-propagate>
            <on-error-propagate type="APIKIT:NOT_IMPLEMENTED">
                <ee:transform>
                    <ee:message>
                        <ee:set-payload><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/json
---
{message: "Not Implemented"}]]></ee:set-payload>
                    </ee:message>
                    <ee:variables>
                        <ee:set-variable variableName="httpStatus">501</ee:set-variable>
                    </ee:variables>
                </ee:transform>
            </on-error-propagate>
        </error-handler>
    </flow>
    <flow name="api-console">
        <http:listener config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/console/*">
            <http:response statusCode="#[vars.httpStatus default 200]">
                <http:headers>#[vars.outboundHeaders default {}]</http:headers>
            </http:response>
            <http:error-response statusCode="#[vars.httpStatus default 500]">
                <http:body>#[payload]</http:body>
                <http:headers>#[vars.outboundHeaders default {}]</http:headers>
            </http:error-response>
        </http:listener>
        <apikit:console config-ref="api-config" />
        <error-handler>
            <on-error-propagate type="APIKIT:NOT_FOUND">
                <ee:transform>
                    <ee:message>
                        <ee:set-payload><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/json
---
{message: "Resource not found"}]]></ee:set-payload>
                    </ee:message>
                    <ee:variables>
                        <ee:set-variable variableName="httpStatus">404</ee:set-variable>
                    </ee:variables>
                </ee:transform>
            </on-error-propagate>
        </error-handler>
    </flow>
    <flow name="get:\users\userbyid:api-config">
        <ee:transform>
            <ee:message>
                <ee:set-payload><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/json
---
[
  {
    id: 3,
    name: "Clementine Bauch",
    username: "Samantha",
    email: "Nathan@yesenia.net",
    address: {
      street: "Douglas Extension",
      suite: "Suite 847",
      city: "McKenziehaven",
      zipcode: "59590-4157",
      geo: {
        lat: "-68.6102",
        lng: "-47.0653"
      }
    },
    phone: "1-463-123-4447",
    website: "ramiro.info",
    company: {
      name: "Romaguera-Jacobson",
      catchPhrase: "Face to face bifurcated interface",
      bs: "e-enable strategic applications"
    }
  }
]]]></ee:set-payload>
            </ee:message>
        </ee:transform>
    </flow>
    <flow name="get:\users:api-config">
        <ee:transform>
            <ee:message>
                <ee:set-payload><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/json
---
[
  {
    id: 1,
    name: "Leanne Graham",
    username: "Bret",
    email: "Sincere@april.biz",
    address: {
      street: "Kulas Light",
      suite: "Apt. 556",
      city: "Gwenborough",
      zipcode: "92998-3874",
      geo: {
        lat: "-37.3159",
        lng: "81.1496"
      }
    },
    phone: "1-770-736-8031 x56442",
    website: "hildegard.org",
    company: {
      name: "Romaguera-Crona",
      catchPhrase: "Multi-layered client-server neural-net",
      bs: "harness real-time e-markets"
    }
  }
]]]></ee:set-payload>
            </ee:message>
        </ee:transform>
    </flow>
</mule>
```

## See Also

- [Error Handling Reference](apikit-error-handling-reference)
---

# Set a SOAP Header

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Add SOAP headers declared in your WSDL to outbound responses from an APIkit for SOAP application.
This walkthrough extends the `OrderTshirt` flow and DataWeave so the response includes header data
such as `APIUsageInformation`. Download `tshirt2.wsdl`, complete
[prerequisites for Using APIkit for SOAP](apikit-4-soap-prerequisites-task), and
[create an APIkit for SOAP project](apikit-4-soap-project-task) before you begin.

1.  In Anypoint Studio, open `tshirt2.wsdl` in `src/main/resources/api` and scroll to the
    `APIUsageInformation` element, which is the element expected by the contract.
2.  On the canvas, select `Transform Message` in the `OrderTshirt:/soapkit-config` flow.

    The following DataWeave code is available:

    ```linenums
    {
    	body : {
    		ns0#OrderTshirtResponse: {
    			orderId: "I got a request from "
    			++ payload.body.ns0#OrderTshirt.name
    			++ " using the following auth header "
    			++ (payload.headers["AuthenticationHeader"].ns0#AuthenticationHeader.apiKey default "")
    		}
    	} write "application/xml"
    }
    ```

3.  Add the following `APIUsageInformation` header declaration:

    ```linenums
    {
    	body : {
    		ns0#OrderTshirtResponse: {
    			orderId: "I got a request from "
    			++ payload.body.ns0#OrderTshirt.name
    			++ " using the following auth header "
    			++ (payload.headers["AuthenticationHeader"].ns0#AuthenticationHeader.apiKey default "")
    		}
    	} write "application/xml",

    	headers: { (1)
    		header: {
    			ns0#APIUsageInformation: {
    			}
    		} write "application/xml"
    	}

    }
    ```

    <table><tbody><tr><td><i data-value="1"></i><b>1</b></td><td>This DataWeave code adds headers to the output of the <code>Transform Message</code> component.</td></tr></tbody></table>

4.  Add the `apiCallsRemaining` header to the `APIUsageInformation` element:

    { body : { ns0#OrderTshirtResponse: { orderId: "I got a request from " ++
    payload.body.ns0#OrderTshirt.name ++ " using the following auth header " ++
    (payload.headers\["AuthenticationHeader"\].ns0#AuthenticationHeader.apiKey default "") } } write
    "application/xml",

        headers: {
        	header: {
        		ns0#APIUsageInformation: {
        			apiCallsRemaining: 10 **(1)**
        		}
        	} write "application/xml"
        }

    }

    <table><tbody><tr><td><i data-value="1"></i><b>1</b></td><td>Set <code>apiCallsRemaining</code> to <code>10</code>.</td></tr></tbody></table>

5.  Save and run the project again.
6.  In SoapUI, execute the `OrderTshirt` request again.

    The response envelope from APIkit for SOAP is:

```xml
<soap:Envelope xmlns:soap="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/">
   <soap:Header>
      <ns0:APIUsageInformation xmlns:ns0="http://mulesoft.org/tshirt-service">
         <apiCallsRemaining>10</apiCallsRemaining>
      </ns0:APIUsageInformation>
   </soap:Header>
   <soap:Body>
      <ns0:OrderTshirtResponse xmlns:ns0="http://mulesoft.org/tshirt-service">
         <orderId>I got a request from John, using the following auth header 987654321</orderId>
      </ns0:OrderTshirtResponse>
   </soap:Body>
</soap:Envelope>
```

## See Also

- [APIkit for SOAP Module Reference](apikit-for-soap-module-reference)
---

# APIkit for SOAP Error Handling Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

APIkit for SOAP maps routing failures and SOAP fault conditions to typed errors you handle in Mule.
The table in this reference lists each APIKIT-SOAP error type and what it indicates. The scaffolder
does not generate default error-handling logic; add `on-error` strategies in your flows as needed.

## APIkit for SOAP Error Types

APIkit for SOAP defines two error types:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Error Type</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>APIKIT-SOAP:ROUTER</p></td><td><p>Routing exception</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>APIKIT-SOAP:FAULT</p></td><td><p>Fault exception</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## APIkit for SOAP Error Handling

APIkit for SOAP scaffolder generates no error-handling code.

## See Also

- [Error Handlers](../../mule-runtime/latest/error-handling)
---

# Find Unimplemented Operations Using a SOAP Fault

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Exercise the `OrderTshirt` operation in SoapUI against a deployed APIkit for SOAP project before
that operation is implemented. The runtime returns a SOAP fault that names the missing router
operation so you know which flow to build in Anypoint Studio. This example requests a large t-shirt
for a customer named `John`, which the stub cannot process yet.

1.  In SoapUI, create a new SOAP project and put the URL for your deployed Mule APIkit project in
    the initial WSDL file.  
    In this example, the URL is `http://localhost:8081/TshirtService/TshirtServicePort?wsdl`.

    ![New SOAP project window displaying the initial WSDL file: http://localhost:8081/TshirtService/TshirtServicePort?wsdl](_images/soapui-new-project.png)

2.  In SoapUI, request a large t-shirt for John by entering some values into the request envelope:
    1.  Select **Projects** > **TshirtServicePort?wsdl** > **TshirtServiceSoapBinding** >
        **OrderTshirt** > **Request 1**.
    2.  Enter **L** between the `<size>` tags.
    3.  Enter **John** between the `<name>` tags.

3.  Submit the request.

    The result is the SOAP fault:

    ```xml
    <soap:Envelope xmlns:soap="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/">
       <soap:Body>
          <soap:Fault xmlns:soap="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope">
             <faultcode>soap:Server</faultcode>
             <faultstring>Operation [OrderTshirt:\soapkit-config] not implemented</faultstring>
          </soap:Fault>
       </soap:Body>
    </soap:Envelope>
    ```

## See Also

- [Return a Valid SOAP Response After a Fault](apikit-4-prevent-fault-task)
---

# Before You Begin Using APIkit for SOAP

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Prepare your workstation and assets before you build a WSDL-based API with APIkit for SOAP. You need
supported Anypoint Studio and Mule runtime versions, the APIkit for SOAP component, a SOAP test
client, and a WSDL file such as the sample used in the SOAP tutorials.

## Choose Your APIkit for SOAP Module Version

Your Mule runtime and JDK determine which APIkit for SOAP dependency to use. Read
[Choose the APIkit for SOAP module version](apikit-4-for-soap#choose-the-apikit-for-soap-module-version)
first, then align your project dependency with that choice.

- APIkit for SOAP 1.7.0 supports JDK 8, 11, and 17 on Mule runtime 4.1.x and later. Use APIkit for
  SOAP 1.7.0 if you must stay on Mule runtime earlier than 4.9.11, or if you√¢¬Ä¬ôre not ready for JDK
  17-only deployments.
- APIkit for SOAP 2.0.0 and later requires JDK 17 and Mule runtime 4.9.11 or later. Use APIkit for
  SOAP 2.0.0 for new Mule projects.

## Prerequisites

- Install the required software:
  - Studio 7.1 or later
  - A supported Mule runtime for the APIkit for SOAP (4.1.x or later for 1.7.0; 4.9.11 or later for
    2.0.0)
  - The APIkit for SOAP module version that matches your Mule runtime and JDK (1.7.0 or 2.0.0)

    Studio includes the Anypoint APIkit SOAP extension.

- Download and install a SOAP client.

  For example, [SoapUI](https://www.soapui.org/downloads/soapui.html).

- Download or obtain the URL for a WSDL file.

  For example, [WSDL tshirt2.wsdl](_attachments/tshirt2.wsdl).

## See Also

- [Implementing a SOAP Interface](implementing-apikit-4-for-soap)
---

# Create an APIkit for SOAP Project

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Create a Mule project from a WSDL so Anypoint Studio generates APIkit for SOAP flows and stores the
contract under `src/main/resources/api`. Meet the
[prerequisites for using APIkit for SOAP](apikit-4-soap-prerequisites-task) first. After you pick
the service and port, run the application and retrieve the published WSDL from the HTTP endpoint to
verify the service.

1.  In Anypoint Studio, from **File**, select **New** > **Mule Project**.

    ![New Mule project window that highlights the project-name field, runtime engine, *From Design Center or Local Files* tab, and expand icon for browsing files](_images/create-new-mule-project-soap.png)

<table><tbody><tr><td><i data-value="1"></i><b>1</b></td><td>In <strong>Project Name</strong>, enter a name for the project.<br>For example, <code>soap-implementation</code>.</td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="2"></i><b>2</b></td><td>Select the Mule runtime engine version.<br>For example, <code>Mule Server 4.2.2 EE</code>.</td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="3"></i><b>3</b></td><td>In <strong>API Specification</strong>, select <strong>From Design Center or Local Files</strong>.</td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="4"></i><b>4</b></td><td>Select <strong>Browse files</strong> from the <strong>More actions</strong> menu.</td></tr></tbody></table>

2.  Select a service and port. For this example, accept the default values, `TshirtService` and
    `TshirtServicePort`.

    ![API specification window that highlights the service and the port from the menu](_images/create-apikit-soap-project.png)

3.  Click **Finish**.

    The new project appears with the WSDL file in the `src/main/resources/api` folder.

4.  Right-click a blank area of the canvas and select **Run Project**.
5.  In your SOAP UI client, go to `http://localhost:8081/TshirtService/TshirtServicePort?wsdl`.

    The SOAP service returns the content of your WSDL file.

If you update your WSDL file, regenerate your flows by right-clicking your WSDL file and selecting
**Mule** > **Generate Flows From WSDL**.

## See Also

- [Find Unimplemented Operations Using a SOAP Fault](apikit-4-soap-fault-task)
---

# APIkit for REST Module Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Configure the APIkit for REST module in Mule: declare the Maven dependency, add `apikit:config`, and
place the `apikit:router` and optional console and flow mappings in your XML. This reference lists
dependency coordinates, namespaces, schema locations, and element attributes you use when you
implement or troubleshoot REST APIs.

## APIkit for REST Dependency Information

<dependency>
  <groupId>org.mule.modules</groupId>
  <artifactId>mule-apikit-module</artifactId>
  <version>LATEST\_VERSION</version> **(1)**
  <classifier>mule-plugin</classifier>
</dependency>

<table><tbody><tr><td><i data-value="1"></i><b>1</b></td><td>Check <a href="https://docs.mulesoft.com/release-notes/apikit/apikit-release-notes">APIkit release notes</a> to get the <strong>LATEST_VERSION</strong>.</td></tr></tbody></table>

## APIkit for REST Configuration and Operations

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Element Name</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p><code>apikit:config</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>APIkit configuration</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>apikit:console</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Property of <code>config-ref</code> in the <code>http:listener</code> element in the api-console flow that defines the API console URL</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>apikit:flow-mappings</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Container for one or more <code>apikit:flow-mapping</code> elements that map API resources and actions to flows</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>apikit:flow-mapping</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maps a resource and action from the API specification to a specific Mule flow</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>apikit:router</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>REST routing message processor nested in the flow element</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### apikit:config Attributes

The `apikit:config` attributes are of type `NonBlankString`.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Attribute Name</th><th>Required</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p><code>outboundHeadersMapName</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the map that contains response headers.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>httpStatusVarName</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the HTTP status code in the response.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>keepRamlBaseUri</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Alias for the equivalent <code>keepApiBaseUri</code> attribute.</p></div><div><p>This attribute is deprecated as of version APIkit 1.2.0. Included to provide compatibility with APIkit 1.1.x.<br>Use <code>keepApiBaseUri</code> instead of <code>keepRamlBaseUri</code> in your API implementations.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>name</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configuration name that other elements, such as <code>apikit:router</code>, reference.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>raml</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Alias for the equivalent <code>api</code> attribute.</p></div><div><p>This attribute is deprecated as of version APIkit 1.2.0. Included to provide compatibility with APIkit 1.1.x.<br>Use <code>api</code> instead of <code>raml</code> in your API implementations.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>parser</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Parser used to validate requests.</p></div><div><p>Attribute valid for APIkit 1.2.0 and later.</p></div><div><p>Possible values are:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p><div><p><strong>APIkit Router</strong> starts by using AMF. If it has errors, it starts by using the RAML parser.</p></div></li><li><p>AMF</p><div><p><strong>APIkit Router</strong> uses AMF to read the API and validate incoming requests.</p></div></li><li><p>RAML</p><div><p><strong>APIkit Router</strong> starts by using AMF, and if it has errors, it starts by using the RAML parser.</p></div></li></ul></div><div><p>Default Value: AUTO.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>api</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Root API file location.</p></div><div><p>This attribute is valid for APIkit 1.2.0 and later.</p></div><div><p>The <code>api</code> attribute can be any URL, the relative path to a file located under <code>src/main/resources/api</code>, or the <code>API Sync</code> resource path.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>keepApiBaseUri</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Attribute valid for APIkit 1.2.0 and later.</p></div><div><p>When set to <code>false</code>, APIkit rewrites the <code>baseUri</code> in the RAML definition of the API.<br>If set to <code>true</code>, APIkit does not rewrite the <code>baseUri</code> field.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

If you use APIkit behind a reverse proxy and set `keepRamlBaseUri` to `true`, use **API Console** to
put the URL of a proxy in the `baseUri` property.

### apikit:router Attributes

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Attribute Name</th><th>Required</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p><code>disableValidations</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Disables validations of requests.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>headerStrictValidation</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Validates that request headers are defined in the RAML file or belong to a well-known set of headers.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>queryParamsStrictValidation</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Validates that request query parameters are defined in the RAML file.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### apikit:flow-mapping Attributes

Use the `apikit:flow-mapping` element to route a resource and action defined in the API
specification (RAML or OpenAPI specification) to an existing Mule flow.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Attribute Name</th><th>Required</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p><code>resource</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Resource path from the API specification to map, for example <code>/users</code>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>action</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>HTTP method to map for the resource, for example <code>get</code> or <code>post</code>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>flow-ref</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the Mule flow that handles the mapped resource and action.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>content-type</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Request media type to match, for example <code>application/json</code>. When omitted, the mapping applies to any <code>Content-Type</code> for that resource and action. When set, you can route the same resource and action to different flows depending on the request media type.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

In APIkit configuration, nest `apikit:flow-mapping` in `apikit:flow-mappings`, and nest
`apikit:flow-mappings` in `apikit:config`.

```xml
<apikit:config name="api-config" api="api/api.raml">
  <apikit:flow-mappings>
    <apikit:flow-mapping resource="/orders" action="post"
        content-type="application/json"
        flow-ref="process-json-order"/>
    <apikit:flow-mapping resource="/orders" action="post"
        content-type="application/xml"
        flow-ref="process-xml-order"/>
  </apikit:flow-mappings>
</apikit:config>
```

## APIkit for REST Namespace and Schema

xmlns:apikit="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/mule-apikit"
xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/mule-apikit
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/mule-apikit/current/mule-apikit.xsd"

## APIkit for REST System Properties

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>System Property</th><th>Default</th><th>Description</th><th>Since</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p><code>mule.xml.expandExternalEntities</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Allows parser to expand XML external entities. The default value is <code>false</code> to prevent attacks.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>1.3.7</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>mule.xml.expandInternalEntities</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Allows parser to expand XML internal entities. The default value is <code>false</code> to prevent attacks.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>1.3.7</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>apikit.console.disabled</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Disables console.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>1.3.7</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>apikit.console.stream.amf.model</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Streams AMF parser model to the console.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>1.5.0</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>raml.json_schema.fail_on_warning</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Fails when there is a <code>warning</code> while validating a payload when the RAML parser is configured.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>1.1.15</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>yagi.json_duplicate_keys_detection</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Checks no duplicate JSON object field names are encountered when the RAML parser is configured.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>1.1.15</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>yagi.json_block_unsafe_polymorphic_base_types</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Blocks the use of unsafe base types when the RAML parser is configured.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>1.5.0</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>mule.apikit.parser</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AUTO</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Overrides parser type. Possible values are:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AMF</p></li><li><p>RAML</p></li><li><p>AUTO</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>1.3.7</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>apikit.multipart.size.limit</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>268435456</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Limits the size of the multipart payload to be validated by throwing <code>InvalidFormParameterException</code> if the incoming multipart payload exceeds the limit (expressed in bytes).</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>1.8.0</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>fullDomain</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>localhost</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Overrides the console URL.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>1.1.15</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

- [APIkit for REST](apikit-4-for-rest)
- [Building, Implementing, and Testing a REST API](apikit-4-implement-rest-api)
- [Converting a Mule Project to an APIkit Project](apikit-workflow-convert-existing)
- [APIkit for REST Error Handling Reference](apikit-error-handling-reference)
- [APIkit for REST Scaffolding Reference](apikit-4-scaffolding-reference)
- [APIkit Release Notes](apikit-release-notes)
---

# Using Avro Schemas with APIkit for AsyncAPI

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

APIkit for AsyncAPI accepts [Avro](https://avro.apache.org/) in AsyncAPI message definitions so you
serialize payloads and validate them in Mule. Declare schemas inline in the spec or reference
external Avro documents; Studio can expose message shape during design, and
[DataSense](../../studio/datasense-concept) assists with Avro-backed message metadata.

## Schema Definitions

The Avro spec defines its format by giving the value of `schemaFormat` as
`application/vnd.apache.avro`.

The Avro spec supports describing the schema inline or referencing an external document that
describes the schema in the Avro format:

- Inline definition

  publish: summary: inform details about person operationId: lightMeasured message: schemaFormat:
  'application/vnd.apache.avro;version=1.9.0' payload: name: lightMeasured type: record fields: -
  name: lumens type: int example: 134 - name: sentAt type: string logicalType: timestamp-millis 10
  example: 1985-04-12T23:20:50.52Z

- Reference to an Avro fragment

  channels: smartylighting.streetlights.1.0.event.{streetlightId}.monitor: publish: message:
  schemaFormat: application/vnd.apache.avro;version=1.9.0 payload: $ref:
  /exchange_modules/83589b9b-6630-4c28-985d-174ee9172661/lumenschema/1.0.0/lumens.json'

The `lumens.json` file provides the specification in the Avro schema.

{ "type": "record", "name": "lightMeasured", "fields": \[ {"name": "lumens", "type": "int"},
{"name": "sentAt", "type": {"type": "string", "logicalType": "timestamp-millis"}} \] }

APIkit for AsyncAPI supports async APIs that have Avro messages defined inline. APIkit for AsyncAPI
doesn√¢¬Ä¬ôt support API specs that refer to Avro fragments.

## Avro Message Encoding and Validation

Avro messages are encoded in either binary or text (JSON) format. The specification provides the
value of `contentType` OR `defaultContentType`.

APIkit for AsyncAPI validates messages encoded in text (JSON) but doesn√¢¬Ä¬ôt validate binary-encoded
messages.
---

# APIkit for AsyncAPI Error Handling Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

APIkit for AsyncAPI maps runtime failures to typed errors that cover validation, publishing,
routing, and internal faults. Use the table to diagnose which phase failed and adjust your AsyncAPI
implementation or flow configuration.

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Error Type</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR</p></td><td><p>Internal error.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>VALIDATION_ERROR</p></td><td><p>The payload doesn√¢¬Ä¬ôt meet the schema of the API spec.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>PUBLISHING_ERROR</p></td><td><p>APIkit fails to publish messages on the selected channel.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>ROUTING_ERROR</p></td><td><p>APIkit fails to route messages to the flows.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## APIkit for AsyncAPI Error Handling

The APIkit for AsyncAPI scaffolder does not generate error-handling code. Add `on-error` components
and scopes in your Mule application as needed.
---

# APIkit for AsyncAPI Module Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Integrate AsyncAPI specifications into Mule with the APIkit for AsyncAPI module: add the Maven
dependency, declare the module namespace, and apply the XML schema when you configure generated
scaffolding. This reference documents coordinates, namespaces, schema locations, and configuration
elements you use to implement event-driven APIs.

## Dependencies

<dependency>
  <groupId>org.mule.modules</groupId>
  <artifactId>mule-asynckit-module</artifactId>
  <version>LATEST\_VERSION</version> **(1)**
  <classifier>mule-plugin</classifier>
</dependency>

<table><tbody><tr><td><i data-value="1"></i><b>1</b></td><td>Check the <a href="apikit-release-notes">APIkit Release Notes</a> to get the <strong>LATEST_VERSION</strong>.</td></tr></tbody></table>

## Namespace and Schema

xmlns:apikit-asyncapi="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/apikit-asyncapi"
xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/apikit-asyncapi
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/apikit-asyncapi/current/mule-apikit-asyncapi.xsd">

## Configurations

### Config

Default configuration

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Api Definition</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>API definition.</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Disable Content Validation</p></td><td><p>Boolean</p></td><td><p>Disable payload content validation.</p></td><td><p><code>false</code></p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Kafka Configs</p></td><td><p>Array of <a href="#KafkaConfig">Kafka Config</a></p></td><td><p>List of Kafka server configurations. One for each Kafka server listed in the specification that is scaffolded to create the app.</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Anypoint MQ Configs</p></td><td><p>Array of <a href="#AnypointMqConfig">Anypoint MQ Config</a></p></td><td><p>List of AMQ configurations.</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Solace Configs</p></td><td><p>Array of <a href="#SolaceConfig">Solace Config</a></p></td><td><p>List of Solace configurations.</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Salesforce Pub/Sub Configs</p></td><td><p>Array of <a href="#SalesforcePubSubConfig">Salesforce Pub/Sub Connector Config</a></p></td><td><p>List of SalesforcePubSub configurations.</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Identifier of this element used to reference it in other components.</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Supported Message Brokers

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Message Broker</th><th>Description</th><th>Supported Protocols</th><th>Configuration Details</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Anypoint MQ</p></td><td><p>Supports asynchronous messaging with Anypoint MQ.</p></td><td><p>anypointmq</p></td><td><p>Anypoint MQ configurations are specified in the server object of the AsyncAPI specification. Supports <strong>Message Listener</strong> and <strong>Subscribe</strong> operations.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Kafka</p></td><td><p>Supports asynchronous messaging with Apache Kafka.</p></td><td><p>kafka, kafka-secure</p></td><td><p>Kafka configurations are specified in the server object of the AsyncAPI specification. Supports <strong>Message Listener</strong> and <strong>Subscribe</strong> operations.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Salesforce platform events</p></td><td><p>Allows implementing an Async API specification using Anypoint Connector for Salesforce Pub/Sub (Salesforce Pub/Sub Connector).</p></td><td><p>salesforcepubsub*</p></td><td><p>Connection details are available in the properties file. Supports <strong>Message Listener</strong> and <strong>Subscribe</strong> operations, and <strong>Message Listener</strong> and <strong>Publish</strong> operations.</p><p>In the <strong>Message Listener</strong> operation, the AsyncAPI spec <code>defaultContentType</code> must be application/JSON.</p><p>In the <strong>Publish</strong> operation, the payload must be JSON. The APIkit for AsyncAPI module transforms the JSON payload to the array-of-objects type.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Solace Pub/Sub+</p></td><td><p>Supports the implementation of an Async API specification using Solace PubSub+ Connector.</p></td><td><p>solace</p></td><td><p>Connection details are available in the properties file. Supports <strong>Message Listener</strong> and <strong>Publish</strong> operations.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

\* You must use Anypoint Connector for Salesforce Pub/Sub Connector 1.0.15 or later.

#### Associated Operations

- [Publish](#Publish)

#### Associated Sources

- [Message Listener](#MessageListener)

## Validation of Messages

Unless explicitly restricted, the JSON schema validation allows omitting and adding fields. Check
the message validation against the schema in the following table:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Schema Definition</th><th>Message</th><th>Valid</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>{ A: integer, B: string }</p></td><td><p>{A, B}</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>{ A: integer, B: string }</p></td><td><p>{C, D}</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>{ A: integer, B: string } required [A, B]</p></td><td><p>{C, D}</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>{ A: integer, B: string } required [A, B]</p></td><td><p>{A, B, C}</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>{ A: integer, B: string } required [A, B] <code>additionalProperties</code> = <code>false</code></p></td><td><p>{A, B, C}</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>{ A: integer, B: string } required [A, B] <code>additionalProperties</code> = <code>false</code></p></td><td><p>{A, B}</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

- The `required` field specifies the list of fields that are mandatory in the JSON object.
- The `additionalProperties` field specifies if you can include additional properties to the JSON
  object.

The schemas must specify `required` and `additionalProperties` in the API specification.

> [!NOTE] The field value is checked against the type specified in the schema as part of the
> validation.

If an operation has multiple message schemas associated in the specification, the validation adheres
to these principles:

- Messages are validated against every schema.
- If it√¢¬Ä¬ôs compliant with at least one schema, the message is considered valid.
- If the message doesn√¢¬Ä¬ôt adhere to at least one schema, the message is considered invalid.

### Example

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Schema Definition</th><th>Message</th><th>Valid</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>{ A: integer, B: string } required [A, B] <code>additionalProperties</code> = <code>false</code> { C: integer, D: string } required [C, D] <code>additionalProperties</code> = <code>false</code></p></td><td><p>{A, B}</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>{ A: integer, B: string } required [A, B] <code>additionalProperties</code> = <code>false</code> { C: integer, D: string } required [C, D] <code>additionalProperties</code> = <code>false</code></p></td><td><p>{B, C}</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>{ A: integer, B: string } required [A, B] <code>additionalProperties</code> = <code>false</code> { C: integer, D: string } required [C, D] <code>additionalProperties</code> = <code>false</code></p></td><td><p>{A, B, C}</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>{ A: integer, B: string } required [A, B] <code>additionalProperties</code> = <code>false</code> { C: integer, D: string } required [C, D] <code>additionalProperties</code> = <code>false</code></p></td><td><p>{C, D}</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Operations

## Publish

`<apikit-asyncapi:publish>`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><p>Binary</p></td><td><p>The binary content to be processed</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Disable Content Validation</p></td><td><p>Boolean</p></td><td><p>Indicates if validation of the content is disabled</p></td><td><p><code>False</code></p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Channel Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Channel name</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Server Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Server name</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of a variable in which the operation output is placed</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Expression that is evaluated against the operation output and the result of that expression is stored in the target variable</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></td><td><p>Set of error mappings</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#PublishResponse">Publish Response</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#Config)

### Throws

- APIKIT-ASYNCAPI:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- APIKIT-ASYNCAPI:PUBLISHING_ERROR
- APIKIT-ASYNCAPI:VALIDATION_ERROR

## Sources

## Message Listener

`<apikit-asyncapi:message-listener>`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Disable Content Validation</p></td><td><p>Boolean</p></td><td><p>Disable payload content validation</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Channel Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Channel name</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Servers</p></td><td><p>Array of String</p></td><td><p>List of servers to listen to</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output MIME Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>MIME type of the payload that this operation generates</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Encoding</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Encoding of the payload that this operation generates</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Primary Node Only</p></td><td><p>Boolean</p></td><td><p>Specifies if this source must be executed on the primary node when running in a cluster</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Specify and configure repeatable streams</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Redelivery Policy</p></td><td><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicy">Redelivery Policy</a></p></td><td><p>Policy for processing the redelivery of the same message</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any type</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#KafkaMessageAttributes">Kafka Message Attributes</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#AnypointMqMessageAttributes">Anypoint MQ Message Attributes</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#SolaceMessageAttributes">Solace Message Attributes</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#SalesforcePubSubAttributes">Salesforce Pub/Sub Attributes</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#Config)

## Types

### Kafka Config

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Server Key</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Identifier for the server configuration within the AsyncAPI configuration</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Producer Config Ref</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Reference to the Kafka Producer configuration</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Consumer Config Ref</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Reference to the Kafka Consumer configuration</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

> [!NOTE] The Server Key is required to uniquely identify the server configuration. The references
> for Producer and Consumer configurations are optional and depend on the specific setup and
> requirements of the Kafka implementation.

### Anypoint MQ Config

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Server Key</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Identifier for the server configuration in AsyncAPI</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Reference to the Anypoint MQ configuration details</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

> [!NOTE] The Server Key is required to uniquely identify the server configuration. Config Ref is
> optional and used to specify additional configuration details.

### Solace Config

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Server Key</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Server key set in the AsyncAPI spec</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Reference to the configuration name of the Solace connection</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

> [!NOTE] The Server Key is required to uniquely identify the server configuration. Config Ref is
> optional and used to specify additional configuration details.

### Limitations of Solace PubSub+ Connector

Solace PubSub+ Connector in APIkit for AsyncAPI has these limitations:

- The Solace PubSub+ Connector supports `queue` and `topic` for `destinations` in the **message
  listener** and **publish** operations. But the APIkit for AsyncAPI implementation only supports
  using `queue` as the value of `destination`.
- Only one operator of the same type must be mapped to the same `queue` of `destination`.

### Salesforce Pub/Sub Connector Config

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Server Key</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Server key set in the AsyncAPI spec</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Reference to the name of the Salesforce Pub/Sub Connector configuration</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

> [!NOTE] The Server Key is required to uniquely identify the server configuration. Config Ref is
> optional and used to specify additional configuration details.

### Repeatable In Memory Stream

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Initial Buffer Size</p></td><td><p>Number</p></td><td><p>Amount of memory allocated to consume the stream and provide random access to it. If the stream data is larger than the buffer capacity, it expands according to the <code>bufferSizeIncrement</code> attribute, with an upper limit of <code>maxInMemorySize</code>.</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Buffer Size Increment</p></td><td><p>Number</p></td><td><p>How much the buffer size expands if it exceeds its initial size. A value of zero or lower specifies that the buffer doesn√¢¬Ä¬ôt expand. When the buffer is full, a <code>STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED</code> error occurs.</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Buffer Size</p></td><td><p>Number</p></td><td><p>Maximum amount of memory used. If more is used, a <code>STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED</code> error is raised. A value lower or equal to zero means no limit.</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Buffer Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>These attributes are expressed in this unit.</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable File Store Stream

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>In Memory Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines the maximum memory that the stream uses to keep data in memory. If it consumes more, the stream buffers the content on the disk.</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Buffer Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The unit for expressing <code>maxInMemorySiz</code>.</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Redelivery Policy

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Max Redelivery Count</p></td><td><p>Number</p></td><td><p>Maximum number of times a message can be redelivered and processed unsuccessfully before triggering process-failed-message</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message Digest Algorithm</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Secure hashing algorithm</p></td><td><p>SHA-256</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message Identifier</p></td><td><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicyMessageIdentifier">Redelivery Policy Message Identifier</a></p></td><td><p>Strategy used to identify the messages</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Object Store</p></td><td><p>ObjectStore</p></td><td><p>Object store where the redelivery counter for each message is stored</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Redelivery Policy Message Identifier

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Use Secure Hash</p></td><td><p>Boolean</p></td><td><p>Whether to use a secure hash algorithm to identify a redelivered message.</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Id Expression</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Expression used to determine if a message has been redelivered. You can set this property if <code>useSecureHash</code> is <code>false</code>.</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Publish Response

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Response Data</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td><td><p>Data the server returns in response to a request</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Error Mapping

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Source</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ANY</p></li><li><p>REDELIVERY_EXHAUSTED</p></li><li><p>TRANSFORMATION</p></li><li><p>EXPRESSION</p></li><li><p>SECURITY</p></li><li><p>CLIENT_SECURITY</p></li><li><p>SERVER_SECURITY</p></li><li><p>ROUTING</p></li><li><p>CONNECTIVITY</p></li><li><p>RETRY_EXHAUSTED</p></li><li><p>TIMEOUT</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Type of error encountered during the operation</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The specific component or module where the error needs to be addressed</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Kafka Message Attributes

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Channel Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the Kafka channel (topic) where the message is published or consumed</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Channel Parameters</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td><td><p>Additional parameters or settings specific to the channel</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Creation Timestamp</p></td><td><p>DateTime</p></td><td><p>Timestamp when the message was created</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Metadata associated with the message, typically key-value pairs</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This key determines the partition within the Kafka topic where the
