message is sent</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Leader Epoch</p></td><td><p>Number</p></td><td><p>Epoch of the leader broker at the time the message is sent</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Log Append Timestamp</p></td><td><p>DateTime</p></td><td><p>Timestamp when the message is appended to the log</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Offset</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Message offset in the Kafka topic partition</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Partition</p></td><td><p>Number</p></td><td><p>Partition within the Kafka topic where the message is stored</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Protocol</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Protocol the Kafka server uses</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Server Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the Kafka server that processes the message</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Anypoint MQ Message Attributes

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Ack Token</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Token used for acknowledging the receipt of a message</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Channel Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the Anypoint MQ channel where the message is published or consumed</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Channel Parameters</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td><td><p>Additional parameters or settings specific to the channel</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>MIME type of the message content</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delivery Count</p></td><td><p>Number</p></td><td><p>Number of times the message is delivered</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Headers</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td><td><p>Metadata associated with the message, typically key-value pairs</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message Id</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Unique identifier for the message</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Properties</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td><td><p>Custom properties associated with the message</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Protocol</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Protocol the Anypoint MQ server uses</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Server Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the Anypoint MQ server that processes the message</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Solace Message Attributes

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>DMQ Eligible</p></td><td><p>Boolean</p></td><td><p>Indicates if the message is eligible for Dead Message Queue</p></td><td><p><code>False</code></p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Channel Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the Solace channel where the message is published or consumed</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Channel Parameters</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td><td><p>Additional parameters or settings specific to the Solace channel</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>MIME type of the message content</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delivery Mode</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Delivery mode of the message</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Discard Indication</p></td><td><p>Boolean</p></td><td><p>Indicates if the message is discarded</p></td><td><p><code>False</code></p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Eliding Eligible</p></td><td><p>Boolean</p></td><td><p>Indicates if the message is eligible for eliding</p></td><td><p><code>False</code></p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Endpoint Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Type of endpoint used</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message Reference Id</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Reference identifier for the message</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Priority</p></td><td><p>Number</p></td><td><p>Priority level of the message</p></td><td><p>0</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Protocol</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Protocol the Solace server uses</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Receive Timestamp</p></td><td><p>DateTime</p></td><td><p>Timestamp when the message was received</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Redelivered</p></td><td><p>Boolean</p></td><td><p>Indicates if the message is redelivered</p></td><td><p><code>False</code></p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reply</p></td><td><p>Boolean</p></td><td><p>Indicates if the message expects a reply</p></td><td><p><code>False</code></p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Server Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the Solace server that processes the message</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Time To Live</p></td><td><p>Number</p></td><td><p>Time in milliseconds that the message remains valid</p></td><td><p>0</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Salesforce Pub/Sub Attributes

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Channel Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the Salesforce Pub/Sub channel where the message is published or consumed</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Channel Parameters</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td><td><p>Additional parameters or settings specific to the Salesforce Pub/Sub channel</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Protocol</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Protocol the Salesforce Pub/Sub server uses</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Server Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the Salesforce Pub/Sub server that processes the message</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>
---

# APIkit Compatibility

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

APIkit modules support Mule 4. Review protocol and language coverage in the tables on this page when
you plan AsyncAPI, gRPC, GraphQL, OData, REST, or SOAP APIs. For APIkit versions that align with
[FIPS 140-2](../../mule-runtime/latest/fips-140-2-compliance-support) expectations, see
[APIkit Release Notes](apikit-release-notes).

## APIkit for SOAP Module Versions and Mule Runtime

APIkit for SOAP 1.7.0 provides broad Mule runtime compatibility. APIkit for SOAP 2.0.0 is the major
successor to 1.6.10 and requires JDK 17 and Mule runtime 4.9.11 or later. APIkit for SOAP 1.6.5
through 1.6.10 are deprecated.

### APIkit for SOAP Versions

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Line</th><th>Version</th><th>Minimum Platform</th><th>Use Cases</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Legacy compatibility</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>1.7.0</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>JDK 8, 11, or 17; Mule runtime 4.1.x and later</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>You must keep existing deployments on earlier Mule or Java stacks without adopting the 2.x requirements.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Modern major</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>2.0.0 (successor to 1.6.10)</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>JDK 17 only; Mule runtime 4.9.11 and later</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>You standardize on current Mule and Java 17. You choose the version that receives new features.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Deprecated</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>1.6.5 √¢¬Ä¬ì 1.6.10</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Don√¢¬Ä¬ôt adopt or standardize on these releases.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Migrate to 1.7.0 or 2.0.0 using the <a href="https://docs.mulesoft.com/apikit/latest/apikit-4-for-soap#choose-the-apikit-for-soap-module-version">version selection guide</a>.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

See
[Choose the APIkit for SOAP module version](apikit-4-for-soap#choose-the-apikit-for-soap-module-version)
and [Before You Begin Using APIkit for SOAP](apikit-4-soap-prerequisites-task) when you plan
upgrades.

## Language and Protocol Support

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Language/protocol</th><th>Version</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>AsyncAPI spec</p></td><td><p>2.6</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Protocol Buffers (gRPC)</p></td><td><p>3</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>OData</p></td><td><p>2.0 and 4.0</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>OpenAPI Specification (OAS)</p></td><td><p>2.0 and 3.0</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>RESTful API Modeling Language (RAML)</p></td><td><p>1.0 and 0.8</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>SOAP</p></td><td><p>1.1 and 1.2</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Next Steps

Depending on the language and protocols you want to use to develop your Mule application, choose the
APIkit module that best suits your needs.

- [APIkit for AsyncAPI](apikit-for-asyncapi)
- [APIkit for gRPC](apikit-for-grpc)
- [APIkit for GraphQL](apikit-4-for-graphql)
- [APIkit for OData V2](apikit-4-for-odatav2)
- [APIkit for OData V4](apikit-4-for-odatav4)
- [APIkit for REST](apikit-4-for-rest)
- [APIkit for SOAP](apikit-4-for-soap)
---

# APIkit for REST Error Handling Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

APIkit for REST maps common HTTP and routing failures to typed `APIKIT` errors you handle in Mule.
Use the error type list and status-code mapping in this reference to interpret failures and
customize responses. The scaffolder can generate baseline error handling that mirrors typical HTTP
status semantics.

## APIkit for REST Error Types

APIkit for REST defines error types that correspond to HTTP semantics:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Error Type</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>APIKIT:BAD_REQUEST</p></td><td><p>Bad request</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>APIKIT:NOT_FOUND</p></td><td><p>Resource not found</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>APIKIT:METHOD_NOT_ALLOWED</p></td><td><p>Method not allowed</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>APIKIT:NOT_ACCEPTABLE</p></td><td><p>Not acceptable</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>APIKIT:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE</p></td><td><p>Unsupported media type</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>APIKIT:NOT_IMPLEMENTED</p></td><td><p>Not implemented</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## APIkit for REST Error Handling

The APIkit scaffolder generates error-handling code based on common HTTP status code responses. The
table maps each status to an error type and default message:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Status Code</th><th>Error Type</th><th>Response Message</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>400</p></td><td><p>APIKIT:BAD_REQUEST</p></td><td><p>Bad request</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>404</p></td><td><p>APIKIT:NOT_FOUND</p></td><td><p>Resource not found</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>405</p></td><td><p>APIKIT:METHOD_NOT_ALLOWED</p></td><td><p>Method not allowed</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>406</p></td><td><p>APIKIT:NOT_ACCEPTABLE</p></td><td><p>Not acceptable</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>415</p></td><td><p>APIKIT:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE</p></td><td><p>Unsupported media type</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>501</p></td><td><p>APIKIT:NOT_IMPLEMENTED</p></td><td><p>Not implemented</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## APIkit for REST Example Error

This sample shows an error handler for an HTTP 400 response, which you can adapt for custom status
codes and messages.

```xml
...
    <on-error-propagate type="APIKIT:BAD_REQUEST" doc:name="On Error Propagate">
        <ee:transform xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core" xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd">
            <ee:message>
                <ee:set-payload><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
    output application/json
    ---
    {message: "Bad request"}]]></ee:set-payload>
            </ee:message>
            <ee:variables>
                <ee:set-variable variableName="httpStatus">400</ee:set-variable>
            </ee:variables>
        </ee:transform>
    </on-error-propagate>
...
```

The main flow transmits an HTTP error response that encompasses an HTTP status code paired with an
associated message expressed in clear and straightforward language. In this example,
`message: "Bad request"`.

## See Also

- [Error Handlers](../../mule-runtime/latest/error-handling)
---

# Bindings and Parametrized Channels in APIkit for AsyncAPI

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

APIkit for AsyncAPI maps AsyncAPI channel bindings to Anypoint connectors such as Anypoint MQ,
Kafka, and Solace PubSub+. Use binding parameters to tune publish and subscribe behavior for each
channel. Parametrized channel names let you reuse one channel pattern across many topics or
destinations without duplicating the specification.

## Supported Bindings

### Anypoint Connector for MQ (Anypoint MQ Connector)

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Binding</th><th>Publish</th><th>Message Listener</th><th>Description</th><th>Reference</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p><code>Destination</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>Destination</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>Destination</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>Destination</code> (queue or exchange) name for this channel. It√¢¬Ä¬ôs optional and defaults to the channel name. Specify it if the channel name differs from the actual <code>destination</code> name. For example, if the channel name isn√¢¬Ä¬ôt a valid <code>destination</code> name in Anypoint MQ.</p></div></div></td><td rowspan="2"><div><div><p>See <a href="https://github.com/asyncapi/bindings/blob/b1e7736071ccddc6c3d652bb2071cad4549f8a08/anypointmq/README.md">Anypoint MQ Bindings</a>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>DestinationType</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>DestinationType</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>DestinationType</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Type of destination (queue or exchange, or FIFO queue). It√¢¬Ä¬ôs optional and defaults to <code>queue</code>. Specifies the messaging model supported by this channel:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Publish/subscribe</p></li><li><p>Point-to-point</p></li><li><p>Strict message ordering</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Kafka Connector

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Binding</th><th>Publish</th><th>Message Listener</th><th>Description</th><th>Reference</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p><code>Key</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>Key</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>Key</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Key associated with the message in this channel. It√¢¬Ä¬ôs optional and defaults to <code>no key</code>. Specifies if the message requires a specific key for partitioning or message grouping.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>See <a href="https://github.com/asyncapi/bindings/blob/master/kafka/README.md">Kafka bindings</a>.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Solace PubSub+ Connector

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Binding</th><th>Publish</th><th>Message Listener</th><th>Description</th><th>Reference</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p><code>deliveryMode</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>deliveryMode</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>deliveryMode</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Solace PubSub+ Connector operates in <code>direct</code> or <code>persistent</code> mode. The default mode is <code>persistent</code>. The <code>deliveryMode</code> parameter determines the quality of service for publishing messages. The <code>Direct</code> mode offers lower latency but doesn√¢¬Ä¬ôt guarantee message delivery. The <code>persistent</code> mode ensures that messages are reliably delivered, even in the event of a broker failure.</p></div></div></td><td rowspan="3"><div><div><p>See <a href="https://github.com/asyncapi/bindings/blob/master/solace/README.md">Solace bindings</a>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>destinationType</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>destinationType</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>destinationType</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Solace PubSub+ Connector supports <code>queue</code> in APIkit for AsyncAPI.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>topicSubscriptions</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Not available</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>topicSubscriptions</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Solace PubSub+ Connector specifies the list of topics the queue subscribes to when the <code>destinationType</code> is <code>queue</code>. If you specify no topic, the <code>queue</code> automatically subscribes to the topic the channel name represents.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

> [!NOTE] Anypoint Connector for Apache Kafka (Apache Kafka Connector) and Solace PubSub+ Connector
> don√¢¬Ä¬ôt support bindings.

## Parametrized Channels

APIkit for AsyncAPI supports parametrized channels so you can define dynamic and reusable
configurations, scale your integrations more effectively, and create consistent configurations
across different parts of the integration.

APIkit for AsyncAPI doesn√¢¬Ä¬ôt support parameters if they√¢¬Ä¬ôre defined in a binding. For example, a
`destination` in AnypointMQ.

This code provides a partial API spec example with a parameterized `destination`:

```yaml
channels:
  orderAcknowledgement:
    description: new Order Acknowledgment
    servers:
      - anypointMQ
    bindings:
      anypointmq:
        destination: "{env}.amq-asyncapi-poc.orderAcknowledgement"
        destinationType: queue
    parameters:
      env:
        $ref: "#/components/parameters/env"
    publish:
      operationId: listen-order-ack
      description: publish a new Order Acknowledgment event to this channel and we will process it
      message:
        $ref: "#/components/messages/orderAckMessage"
```

You must set the parametrized channel at design time. You can√¢¬Ä¬ôt set the parametrized channel at
runtime.

See the complete API example using parametrized channels:

asyncapi: '2.6.0' info: title: AMQ-AsyncAPI-PoC version: '1.0.11' description: Proof of Concept for
Anypoint MQ AsyncAPI interface contact: name: Example Company email: address@example.com tags:

- name: WMS description: Warehouse Management System
- name: PoC description: Proof of Concept
- name: Order Acknowledgement

servers: anypointMQ: url:
https://{region}.anypoint.mulesoft.com/api/{version}/organizations/{organizationId}/environments/{environmentId}
protocol: anypointmq protocolVersion: v1 description: The Anypoint MQ broker variables: region:
$ref: '#/components/serverVariables/region' version: $ref: '#/components/serverVariables/version'
organizationId: $ref: '#/components/serverVariables/organizationId' environmentId: $ref:
'#/components/serverVariables/environmentId'

channels: orderAcknowledgement: description: new Order Acknowledgment servers: - anypointMQ
bindings: anypointmq: destination: '{env}.amq-asyncapi-poc.orderAcknowledgement' destinationType:
queue parameters: env: $ref: '#/components/parameters/env' publish: operationId: listen-order-ack
description: publish a new Order Acknowledgment event to this channel and we will process it
message: $ref: '#/components/messages/orderAckMessage'

components: parameters: env: description: The Mule runtime environment schema: type: string enum: -
dev - tst - acc - prd default: tst serverVariables: region: description: the CloudHub region where
the endpoint is situated enum: - mq-eu-west-1 default: mq-eu-west-1 version: description: the
version of the Anypoint MQ API enum: - v1 default: v1 organizationId: description: the identifier of
our organization (business group) enum: - 7a2b1c3d-4e5f-6g7h-8i9j-0k1l2m3n4o5p environmentId:
description: the identifier of the runtime environment within the organization enum: -
a1b2c3d4-5e6f-7g8h-9i0j-1k2l3m4n5o6p - b2c3d4e5-6f7g-8h9i-0j1k-2l3m4n5o6p -
c3d4e5f6-7g8h-9i0j-1k2l-3m4n5o6p default: a1b2c3d4-5e6f-7g8h-9i0j-1k2l3m4n5o6p messages:
orderAckMessage: name: OrderAckMessage description: an order acknowledgment message. payload: $ref:
'schemas/orderAcknowledgement.json'

> [!NOTE] APIkit for AsyncAPI uses the parametrized channels only on `listeners`.
---

# APIkit for AsyncAPI

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

APIkit for AsyncAPI implements event-driven APIs from AsyncAPI specifications that use asynchronous
messaging protocols. Add and configure module operations in Mule flows so you publish and consume
messages through your chosen bindings. AsyncAPI models reliable, event-based integration among
systems; the APIkit scaffolder produces a minimal Mule implementation you import from
[Anypoint Exchange](../../exchange/) and re-scaffold in
[Anypoint Code Builder](../../anypoint-code-builder/imp-asyncapi) or
[Anypoint Studio](../../studio/import-api-specification-exchange#implement-asyncapi-specifications).
With [message validation](apikit-asyncapi-module-reference#validation-of-messages) enabled, the
module validates `listener` and `publish` payloads against your schemas.

> [!NOTE] APIkit for AsyncAPI supports the
> [AsyncAPI 2.6 spec](https://www.asyncapi.com/blog/release-notes-2.6.0).

## See Also

- [APIkit for AsyncAPI Module Reference](apikit-asyncapi-module-reference)
- [APIkit for AsyncAPI Error Handling Reference](apikit-asyncapi-error-handling-reference)
- [AsyncAPI Specifications](../../design-center/design-async-api)
---

# APIkit for gRPC

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Implement gRPC APIs in Mule projects with APIkit for gRPC by modeling services in Protocol Buffers
(`.proto` files) and extending the scaffolder output with your business logic. Google Remote
Procedure Call (gRPC) runs over HTTP/2 and supports unary calls plus server streaming, client
streaming, and bidirectional streaming for microservices and backend-to-backend traffic.

The APIkit scaffolder generates Mule project structure, routing, and a flow per `.proto` method from
published specifications. Import from Anypoint Exchange or scaffold a gRPC API project in Anypoint
Code Builder, and publish reusable Protobuf assets to Exchange when you share contracts across
teams.

> [!NOTE] APIkit for gRPC requires Mule runtime engine 4.11 or later to provide the necessary HTTP/2
> support. At this time, deployment is supported on Runtime Fabric and Hybrid Standalone (HSA)
> infrastructure.

## See Also

- [gRPC Support on Anypoint Platform](../../release-notes/platform/grpc-support)
- [Implementing a gRPC API Specification with Anypoint Code Builder](../../anypoint-code-builder/imp-implement-grpc-api-spec)
---

# APIkit for SOAP Module Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Configure the APIkit for SOAP module in Mule to route SOAP requests from your WSDL, validate inbound
XML when needed, and emit SOAP responses. This reference documents `soapkit:config`, router
behavior, operations, and related XML elements you use when you implement or troubleshoot SOAP APIs.

## Configurations

### Config

Configuration element for a **SOAP Router**

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Name</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Connectors use this configuration name to reference its configuration.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Soap Version</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>SOAP11</p></li><li><p>SOAP12</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SOAP version of the WSDL.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SOAP11</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Address</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Address of the web service.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>MTOM Enabled</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines if the MTOM protocol manages the attachments.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>false</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Inbound Validation Enabled</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines if the schema of the inbound request is validated.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>false</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Inbound Validation Message Level</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>WARN</p></li><li><p>ERROR</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines the validation level of the inbound requests. If set to <code>ERROR</code>, the validation fails and sends an error to the flow.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>WARN</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>HTTP Status Var Name</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Variable name for the HTTP status.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>httpStatus</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Namespace Prefixes</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Map of custom namespaces prefixes to specify in response.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Expiration Policy</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#expiration-policy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before the run-time considers it eligible for expiration. The platform does not necessarily expire the instance at the exact moment that it becomes eligible. The run-time purges the instances when it considers it appropriate.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>WSDL Location</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>WSDL file URL, remote or local.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Service</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the service.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Port</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the port.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Operations

- [Fault](#soap-fault)
- [Router](#soap-router)

## Operations

### SOAP Fault

`<apikit-soap:fault>`

APIkit for SOAP Fault

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Configuration</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Payload</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Mule payload.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Streaming Strategy</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-stream">Repeatable in memory stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-stream">Repeatable file store stream</a></p></li><li><p>Non repeatable stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configure if repeatable streams and their behavior are used.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Non repeatable stream</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Operation</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An operation defined in the WSDL.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Fault</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A fault defined in the selected operation.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Target Variable</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of a variable on which the operation output is placed.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Target Value</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that is evaluated against the operation output. The effect of the expression is stored in the target variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><p>Binary</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### For Configurations

- [Config](#config)

#### Throws

- APIKIT-SOAP:FAULT
- APIKIT-SOAP:ROUTER

### SOAP Router

`<apikit-soap:router>`

APIkit for SOAP Router

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Configuration</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Message</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Mule payload.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Attributes</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Map of Mule message attributes.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Output Mime Type</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Output Encoding</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Streaming Strategy</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-stream">Repeatable in memory stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-stream">Repeatable file store stream</a></p></li><li><p>Non repeatable stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configure if repeatable streams and their behavior are used.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Non repeatable stream</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Target Variable</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of a variable on which the operation output is placed.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Target Value</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that is evaluated against the operation output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the target variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><p>Binary</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes Type</strong></p></td><td><p><a href="#soap-attributes">SOAP Attributes</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### For Configurations

- [Config](#config)

#### Throws

- APIKIT-SOAP:FAULT
- APIKIT-SOAP:ROUTER

## Types

### Expiration Policy

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Max Idle Time</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A scalar time value for the maximum amount of time a dynamic configuration instance is allowed to be idle before it√¢¬Ä¬ôs considered eligible for expiration</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Time Unit</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A time unit that qualifies the <code>maxIdleTime</code> attribute</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable in Memory Stream

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Initial Buffer Size</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Amount of memory that is allocated to consume the stream and provide random access to it. If the stream contains more data than buffer capacity, then it√¢¬Ä¬ôs expanded according to the <code>bufferSizeIncrement</code> attribute, with an upper limit of <code>maxInMemorySize</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Buffer Size Increment</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Amount the buffer size expands if it exceeds its initial size. A value of zero or lower specifies that the buffer doesn√¢¬Ä¬ôt expand. When the buffer is full, a <code>STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED</code> error occurs.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Max Buffer Size</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum amount of memory used. If more is used, a <code>STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED</code> error is raised. A value lower or equal to zero means no limit.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Buffer Unit</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The unit in which all these attributes are expressed.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable File Store Stream

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>In Memory Size</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines the maximum memory that the stream uses to keep data in memory. If it consumes more, the stream buffers the content on disk.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Buffer Unit</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The unit in which <code>maxInMemorySize</code> is expressed.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### SOAP Attributes

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Protocol Headers</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Protocol headers bundled in the response</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Additional Transport Data</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Additional transport data bundled in the response</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### APIkit for SOAP System Properties

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>System Property</th><th>Default</th><th>Description</th><th>Since</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p><code>mule.soapkit.allowControlCharacters</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Enables the use of XML 1.1 C0 characters.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>1.3.0</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>
---

# Querying a GraphQL API Implementation

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Run sample HTTP POST queries against a packaged APIkit for GraphQL service that keeps an in-memory
blog-style dataset for testing. Download and start
[APIkit for GraphQL application example](_attachments/apikit-graphql-example.jar) before you run the
requests. See [GraphQL over HTTP](https://graphql.org/learn/serving-over-http/) for how POST bodies
encode queries and variables.

The walkthrough uses this schema:

type Post { id: ID! user: User title: String body: String comments: \[Comment\] }

type Comment { id: ID! user: User post: Post body: String replies: \[Comment\] }

type User { id: ID! name: String username: String email: String phone: String website: String
address: Address }

type Address { id: ID! street: String city: String zipcode: String latitude: Float longitude: Float
}

input AddressInput { street: String city: String zipcode: String latitude: Float longitude: Float }

input UserInput { name: String username: String email: String phone: String website: String address:
AddressInput }

type Query { # Looks for a user by ID getUser(id: ID!): User # Looks for a post by ID getPost(id:
ID!): Post # Returns the list of all users allUsers: \[User\] # Returns the list of all posts
allPosts: \[Post\] }

type Mutation { # Creates a new user createUser(user: UserInput!): User! # Creates a new post
createPost(userId: ID!, title: String!, body: String!): Post! # Adds a comment to a post
postComment(userId: ID!, postId: ID!, body: String!): Comment! # Adds a reply to a comment
replyToComment(commentId: ID!, userId: ID!, body: String!): Comment! }

## Request Queries Examples

In the following example, the type **Query** defines the list of queries that the service exposes:

... type Query { # Looks for a user by ID getUser(id: ID!): User # Looks for a post by ID
getPost(id: ID!): Post # Returns the list of all users allUsers: \[User\] # Returns the list of all
posts allPosts: \[Post\] } ...

### Retrieve All Posts

This use case makes a simple request for the data of each existing post:

#### Make a Query

{ allPosts { id title body } }

#### Trigger a Request

curl --location --request POST 'http://localhost:8081/graphql' \\ --header 'Content-Type:
application/json' \\ --data-raw '{"query":"{allPosts{id title body}}","variables":{}}'

#### Get a Response

{ "data": { "allPosts": \[ { "id": "88", "title": "tellus nisi eu orci mauris lacinia sapien quis
libero", "body": "Praesent id massa id nisl venenatis lacinia. Aenean sit amet justo. Morbi ut odio.
Cras mi pede, malesuada in, imperdiet et, commodo vulputate, justo. In blandit ultrices enim. Lorem
ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetuer adipiscing elit. Proin interdum mauris non ligula pellentesque
ultrices. Phasellus id sapien in sapien iaculis congue. Vivamus metus arcu, adipiscing molestie,
hendrerit at, vulputate vitae, nisl." }, { "id": "89", "title": "mauris lacinia sapien quis libero",
"body": "Sed ante. Vivamus tortor. Duis mattis egestas metus. Aenean fermentum. Donec ut mauris eget
massa tempor convallis. Nulla neque libero, convallis eget, eleifend luctus, ultricies eu, nibh.
Quisque id justo sit amet sapien dignissim vestibulum. Vestibulum ante ipsum primis in faucibus orci
luctus et ultrices posuere cubilia Curae; Nulla dapibus dolor vel est. Donec odio justo,
sollicitudin ut, suscipit a, feugiat et, eros." }, ... { "id": "87", "title": "nam tristique",
"body": "Proin eu mi. Nulla ac enim. In tempor, turpis nec euismod scelerisque, quam turpis
adipiscing lorem, vitae mattis nibh ligula nec sem. Duis aliquam convallis nunc. Proin at turpis a
pede posuere nonummy. Integer non velit. Donec diam neque, vestibulum eget, vulputate ut, ultrices
vel, augue. Vestibulum ante ipsum primis in faucibus orci luctus et ultrices posuere cubilia Curae;
Donec pharetra, magna vestibulum aliquet ultrices, erat tortor sollicitudin mi, sit amet lobortis
sapien sapien non mi. Integer ac neque." } \] } }

### Retrieve All Users with Data Loader

This query requests the data of all users, including their address information. This example uses a
Data Loader to fetch the addresses of each user with one request.

#### Make a Query

{ allUsers { name email website address { street city zipcode } } }

#### Trigger a Request

curl --location --request POST 'http://localhost:8081/graphql' \\ --header 'Content-Type:
application/json' \\ --data-raw '{"query":"{allUsers{name email website address{street city
zipcode}}}","variables":{}}'

#### Get a Response

{ "data": { "allUsers": \[ { "name": "Rollin Sebley", "email": "rsebley2f@ucsd.edu", "website":
"homestead.com", "address": { "street": "432 Myrtle Trail", "city": "Pau", "zipcode": "64029 CEDEX
9" } }, { "name": "Bonny Madgewick", "email": "bmadgewick2g@mozilla.com", "website": "utexas.edu",
"address": { "street": "5 David Terrace", "city": "Oelaba", "zipcode": null } }, ... { "name":
"Gretal Titmuss", "email": "gtitmuss2e@soup.io", "website": "creativecommons.org", "address": {
"street": "3201 Gale Place", "city": "Bolo", "zipcode": "4201" } } \] } }

### Retrieve a Post by ID

This query example requests the post data by its ID, including the post comments and the usernames.

#### Make a Query

{ getPost(id: 10) { id user { username } title body comments { user { username } body replies { body
} } } }

#### Trigger a Request

curl --location --request POST 'http://localhost:8081/graphql' \\ --header 'Content-Type:
application/json' \\ --data-raw '{"query":"{getPost(id:10){id user{username}title body
comments{user{username}body replies{body}}}}","variables":{}}'

#### Get a Response

{ "data": { "getPost": { "id": "10", "user": { "username": "bdearelli" }, "title": "id luctus nec
molestie sed justo pellentesque viverra pede ac", "body": "Maecenas tristique, est et tempus semper,
est quam pharetra magna, ac consequat metus sapien ut nunc. Vestibulum ante ipsum primis in faucibus
orci luctus et ultrices posuere cubilia Curae; Mauris viverra diam vitae quam. Suspendisse potenti.
Nullam porttitor lacus at turpis. Donec posuere metus vitae ipsum. Aliquam non mauris.", "comments":
\[ { "user": { "username": "cwalesby1p" }, "body": "Praesent blandit. Nam nulla. Integer pede justo,
lacinia eget, tincidunt eget, tempus vel, pede. Morbi porttitor lorem id ligula. Suspendisse ornare
consequat lectus. In est risus, auctor sed, tristique in, tempus sit amet, sem.", "replies": \[\] },
{ "user": { "username": "rricci2d" }, "body": "Maecenas leo odio, condimentum id, luctus nec,
molestie sed, justo. Pellentesque viverra pede ac diam. Cras pellentesque volutpat dui.", "replies":
\[\] } \] } } }

## Request Mutations Examples

The **Mutation** type defines the list of mutations that the service enables.

... type Mutation { # Creates a new user createUser(user: UserInput!): User! # Creates a new post
createPost(userId: ID!, title: String!, body: String!): Post! # Adds a comment to a post
postComment(userId: ID!, postId: ID!, body: String!): Comment! # Adds a reply to a comment
replyToComment(commentId: ID!, userId: ID!, body: String!): Comment! } ...

### Create a New Post

This example request creates a new post and asks for some information about it.

#### Make a Query

mutation CreatePost($userId: ID!, $title: String!, $body: String!) { createPost(userId:
$userId,title: $title,body: $body) { id user { username } title body comments { body } } }

#### Make a Variable

{ "userId": 42, "title": "GraphQL by example", "body": "Let's talk about GraphQL" }

#### Trigger a Request

curl --location --request POST 'http://localhost:8081/graphql' \\ --header 'Content-Type:
application/json' \\ --data-raw '{"query":"mutation
CreatePost($userId:ID!,$title:String!,$body:String!){createPost(userId:$userId,title:$title,body:$body){id
user{username}title body comments{body}}}","variables":{"userId":42,"title":"GraphQL by
example","body":"Let'\\''s talk about GraphQL"}}'

#### Get a Response

{ "data": { "createPost": { "id": "101", "user": { "username": "tblackmore15" }, "title": "GraphQL
by example", "body": "Let's talk about GraphQL", "comments": \[\] } } }

### Add a Comment to the New Post

This example posts a comment in a reply to the post created in the previous step. It also asks for
the service to return the body of the comment created and the original post with its list of
comments to check that the new one is included.

#### Make a Query

mutation PostComment($userId: ID!, $postId: ID!, $body: String!) { postComment(userId: $userId,
postId: $postId, body: $body) { id body post { id title comments { id user { username } body } } } }

#### Make a Variable

{ "userId": 34, "postId": 101, "body": "Nice post! Thanks for sharing! It's just what I was looking
for" }

#### Trigger a Request

curl --location --request POST 'http://localhost:8081/graphql' \\ --header 'Content-Type:
application/json' \\ --data-raw '{"query":"mutation
PostComment($userId:ID!,$postId:ID!,$body:String!){postComment(userId:$userId,postId:$postId,body:$body){id
body post{id title comments{id
user{username}body}}}}","variables":{"userId":34,"postId":101,"body":"Nice post! Thanks for sharing!
It'\\''s just what I was looking for"}}'

#### Get a Response

{ "data": { "postComment": { "id": "201", "body": "Nice post! Thanks for sharing! It's just what I
was looking for", "post": { "id": "101", "title": "GraphQL by example", "comments": \[ { "id":
"201", "user": { "username": "ekosex" }, "body": "Nice post! Thanks for sharing! It's just what I
was looking for" } \] } } } }

### Reply to the New Comment in the Post

This request example creates a reply in response to the comment posted in the previous step. The
query asks the service to return all the information of the post in addition to the new reply.

#### Make a Query

mutation ReplyToComment($commentId: ID!, $userId: ID!, $body: String!) { replyToComment(commentId:
$commentId, userId: $userId, body: $body) { id body post { id title comments { id body replies { id
user { username } body } } } } }

#### Make a Variable

{ "commentId": 201, "userId": 42, "body": "Thank you!" }

#### Trigger a Request

curl --location --request POST 'http://localhost:8081/graphql' \\ --header 'Content-Type:
application/json' \\ --data-raw '{"query":"mutation
ReplyToComment($commentId:ID!,$userId:ID!,$body:String!){replyToComment(commentId:$commentId,userId:$userId,body:$body){id
body post{id title comments{id body replies{id
user{username}body}}}}}","variables":{"commentId":201,"userId":42,"body":"Thank you!"}}'

#### Get a Response

{ "data": { "replyToComment": { "id": "202", "body": "Thank you!", "post": { "id": "101", "title":
"GraphQL by example", "comments": \[ { "id": "201", "body": "Nice post! Thanks for sharing! It's
just what I was looking for", "replies": \[ { "id": "202", "user": { "username": "tblackmore15" },
"body": "Thank you!" } \] } \] } } } }
---

# Mapping a GraphQL API to Your Data Sources

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Connect APIkit for GraphQL to your runtime by choosing connector listeners, data fetchers, and data
loaders that match how you resolve schema fields. Pick an inbound protocol listener, then attach
fetchers for single fields and loaders when you batch per object type to avoid N+1 calls. Sections
below describe how each source fits into the GraphQL execution path in Mule.

## Select Sources

The module sources vary depending on the implementation of your GraphQL API. APIkit for GraphQL
provides two kinds of sources:

- Data Fetchers
- Data Loaders

Your selection depends on your implementation needs.

GraphQL is transport-layer agnostic, so you choose the main connector listener depending on the
protocol that you want to serve your data.

### Add a Listener

The inbound endpoint that triggers the main application flow is the connector listener specific to
the client-server protocol of your choice. GraphQL often communicates over HTTP, so APIkit
scaffolder generates an HTTP listener by default, but you can choose another source for serving your
data over a different protocol.

### Add a Data Fetcher

Data fetchers are sources of Mule flows. They fulfill the data of a single-object field in a query
result. The combination of the following key parts identify them:

- **Object type name**: One of the object types in your schema
- **Field name**: One of the fields that are part of the object type

You can add data fetchers for every object field defined on the schema, regardless of the field
type. If the Mule application does not have a data fetcher configured for a field, the parent type
populates the field value. Otherwise, the client-requested data is not fulfilled, resulting in
`null`.

### Add a Data Loader

As data fetchers, data loaders are also sources of Mule flows. They batch requests related to an
object type. Unlike data fetchers, a unique key identifies them:

- **Object type name**: One of the object types in your schema

Data loaders solve the optimization issue of the N+1 requests when implementing GraphQL APIs. This
issue occurs when N additional query statements are executed to fetch data that could have been
retrieved when performing the first query.

At run time, the module looks for a data fetcher first. If the data fetcher is not available, the
module looks for a data loader. Specifically, in case a data fetcher and a data loader exist for the
same object type, the module uses the data fetcher as the first choice for resolving a field. This
process can impact your API performance because the data fetcher keeps getting invoked N+1 times.

## Choose Operations

The module provides a set of operations that are fundamental for the correct design and execution of
a GraphQL API implementation:

- Route
- Serialize
- Serialize Batch

### Route a Query

The **Route** operation is the entry point of a GraphQL service. It parses, validates, and executes
the query by traversing the graph and calling the data fetcher and data loaders required to fulfill
the query result.

### Serialize a Data Fetcher

The **Serialize** operation occurs at the end of a flow that has a data fetcher source. The same
pair of keys that identify the data fetcher (object type name and field name) identifies it.

### Serialize Batch a Data Loader

The **Serialize Batch** operation occurs at the end of a flow that has a data loader source. The
same key that identifies the data loader (object type name) identifies it.

**Serialize** and **Serialize Batch** operations leverage the use of data sense at design time.
---

# APIkit for GraphQL Module Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Integrate GraphQL APIs in Mule with the APIkit for GraphQL module: reference the GraphQL schema
artifact, tune router limits, and wire `Route`, `Serialize`, and data fetcher or loader sources.
Later sections document configuration parameters, operations, and sources for the XML you generate
or edit after scaffolding.

## Configurations

### Config

Default configuration

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Description</p></td><td><p>Default Value</p></td><td><p>Required</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>API definition</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Defines the path to the API definition resource, for example: <code>resource::7ddfc767-c5b3-4583-9997-a468e251acd2:posts:1.0.0:graphql:zip:api.graphql</code></p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Introspection Enabled</p></td><td><p>Boolean</p></td><td><p>Enables schema introspection.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Query Depth Allowed</p></td><td><p>Number</p></td><td><p>The maximum depth a query can have. The value must be greater than 1.</p></td><td><p>100</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Query Complexity Allowed</p></td><td><p>Number</p></td><td><p>The maximum number of data fields a query can include. The value must be greater than 1.</p></td><td><p>100</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The identifier of this element is used to reference it in other components.</p></td><td><p>None</p></td><td><p>Yes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Operations

- [Route](#route)
- [Serialize](#serialize)
- [Serialize Batch](#serialize-batch)

#### Associated Sources

- [Data Fetcher](#data-fetcher)
- [Data Loader](#data-loader)

## Operations

### Route

`<graphql-router:route>`

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Configuration</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Content</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Content of the message (usually, the payload)</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Output MIME Type</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Output Encoding</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The encoding of the payload that this operation outputs</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Config Ref</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration you use to execute this component</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Streaming Strategy</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-stream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-stream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>Non-Repeatable Stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configure repeatable streaming strategy</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Target Variable</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of a variable on which the operation output is placed</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Target Value</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that is evaluated against the operation output and the effect of that expression is stored in the target variable</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Configurations

- [Config](#config)

### Serialize

`<graphql-router:serialize>`

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Configuration</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration you use</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Inbound Content</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Content of the message (usually, the payload)</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Config Ref</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration you use to execute this component</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Object Type Name</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The object-type name</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Field Name</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The field name</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Target Variable</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of a variable on which the operation output is placed</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Target Value</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that is evaluated against the operation output and the effect of that expression is stored in the target variable</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Configurations

- [Config](#config)

### Serialize Batch

`<graphql-router:serialize-batch>`

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Configuration</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration you use</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Object Type Name</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The object-type name</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Inbound Content</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Content of the message (usually, the payload)</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Config Ref</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration you use to execute this component</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Target Variable</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of a variable on which the operation output is placed</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Target Value</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that is evaluated against the operation output and the effect of that expression is stored in the target variable</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Configurations

- [Config](#config)

## Sources

### Data Fetcher

`<graphql-router:data-fetcher>`

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Configuration</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration you use</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Output MIME Type</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Output Encoding</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The encoding of the payload that this operation outputs</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Config Ref</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration you use to execute this component</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Primary Node Only</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether this source must only be executed on the primary node when running in a cluster</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Streaming Strategy</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-stream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-stream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>Non-Repeatable Stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configure repeatable streaming strategy</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Redelivery Policy</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#redelivery-policy">Redelivery Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines a policy for processing the redelivery of the same message</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Object Type Name</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The object-type name</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Field Name</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The field name</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Body Content</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Content of the message (usually, the payload)</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes Type</strong></p></td><td><p>* <a href="#data-fetcher-attributes">Data Fetcher Attributes</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>
