### Data Loader

`<graphql-router:data-loader>`

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Configuration</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration you use</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Object Type Name</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The object-type name</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Config Ref</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration you use to execute this component</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Primary Node Only</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether this source should only be executed on the primary node when running in a cluster</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Redelivery Policy</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#redelivery-policy">Redelivery Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines a policy for processing the redelivery of the same message</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Body Content</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Content of the message (usually, the payload)</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><p>Array of Any</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Configurations

- [Config](#config)

## Types

### Data Fetcher Attributes

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Alias</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Alias of the queried field</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Arguments</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Arguments of the queried field</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Selected Fields</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Map of subfields selected from this compound type</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Source</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Actual value of the fetched field parent</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable in Memory Stream

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Initial Buffer Size</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The amount of memory that is allocated to consume the stream and provide random access to it. If the stream contains more data than the buffer capacity, then it is expanded according to the <code>bufferSizeIncrement</code> attribute, with an upper limit of <code>maxInMemorySize</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Buffer Size Increment</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How much buffer size expands if it exceeds its initial size. A value of zero or lower specifies that the buffer doesn√¢¬Ä¬ôt expand. When the buffer is full, a <code>STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED</code> error occurs.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Max Buffer Size</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The maximum amount of memory used. If more is used, a <code>STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED</code> error is raised. A value lower or equal to zero means no limit.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Buffer Unit</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>These attributes are expressed in this unit.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable File Store Stream

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>In Memory Size</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines the maximum memory that the stream uses to keep data in memory. If it consumes more, the stream buffers the content on disk.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Buffer Unit</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The unit for expressing <code>maxInMemorySize</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Redelivery Policy

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Max Redelivery Count</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The maximum number of times a message can be redelivered and processed unsuccessfully before triggering a "Process failed" message.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Message Digest Algorithm</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The secure hashing algorithm to use. If not set, the default is SHA-256.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SHA-256</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Message Identifier</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#redelivery-policy-message-identifier">Redelivery Policy Message Identifier</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The strategy to use to identify the messages</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Object Store</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ObjectStore</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The object store where the redelivery counter for each message is stored</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Redelivery Policy Message Identifier

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Use Secure Hash</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether to use a secure hash algorithm to identify a redelivered message</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Id Expression</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expressions to use to determine when a message has been redelivered. You can only set this property if useSecureHash is false.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>
---

# Defining EDM with RAML in APIkit for OData v2

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Define your OData Entity Data Model (EDM) in RAML so APIkit for OData v2 can build the `odata.raml`
library the extension uses to scaffold flows. Model entities with EDM-compatible types, keys, and
annotations that match the requirements below. After scaffolding with the APIkit OData extension,
implement the generated endpoints with your business logic.

## Entity Data Model

The APIkit OData Extension builds the OData API based on an `odata.raml` file that describes the
OData entity data model (EDM) using supported EDM types. The `odata.raml` file is a RAML Library.
Each DataType represents an EntityModel. For example:

```yaml
#%RAML 1.0 Library
uses:
  odata: !include libraries/odata.raml
types:
  Employee:
    properties:
      id:
        type: integer
        (odata.key): bool
        (odata.nullable): bool
      name:
        type: string
```

Annotate at least one of the properties on each entity using the annotation (odata.key).

These annotations pertain to each property:

```yaml
(odata.precision): int
(odata.scale): int
```

Use the optional (odata.remote) annotation to provide the name of the entity in the remote data
source:

```yaml
types:
  Employee:
  (odata.remote): SAP_VBAK
    properties:
      ...
```

## Supported EDM Data Types

The APIkit OData Extension supports all EDM types except Edm.Sbyte. In your model, you define the
RAML types that map to the EDM type shown in the following list of supported EDM types:

### Edm.String

The String type represents fixed or variable length character data.

RAML `type: string`

### Edm.Boolean

The Boolean data type is used to represent the mathematical concept of binary valued logic. There
are no applicable facets for this type.

RAML `type: boolean`

### Edm.Double

The Double type represents a floating point number with 15 digits precision that can represent
values with an approximate range of √Ç¬± 2.23e -308 through √Ç¬± 1.79e +308.

RAML `type: number`

### Edm.Single

The Single type represents a floating point number with seven digits precision that can represent
values with an approximate range of √Ç¬± 1.18e -38 through √Ç¬± 3.40e +38

RAML `type: number`

RAML `format: float`

### Edm.Binary

The binary data type is used to represent fixed or variable length binary data.

RAML `type: file`

### Edm.DateTime

The DateTime type represents date and time with values ranging from 12:00:00 midnight, January 1,
1753 A.D. through 11:59:59 P.M, December 31, 9999 A.D.

RAML `type: datetime-only`

### Edm.Int32

The Int32 type represents a signed 32-bit integer value.

RAML `type: integer`

### Edm.Int64

The Int64 type represents a signed 64-bit integer value.

RAML `type: integer`

RAML `format: int64`

### Edm.Int16

The Int16 type represents a signed 16-bit integer value.

RAML `type: integer`

RAML `format: int16`

### Edm.Byte

The Byte type represents an unsigned 8-bit integer value.

RAML `type: integer`

RAML `format: int8`

### Edm.Decimal

The Decimal type represents numeric values with fixed precision and scale. You can specify the
required precision and scale by modifying these optional properties. This type can describe a
numeric value ranging from negative 10^255 + 1 to positive 10^255 -1.

RAML `type: number`

RAML `(odata.precision): 3`

RAML `(odata.scale): 3`

### Edm.Guid

The globally unique identifier (GUID) type represents a 16-byte (128-bit) number compliant with the
UUID standard (RFC 4122).

RAML `type: string`

RAML `(odata.type): guid`

### Edm.Time

The Time type represents the time of day with values ranging from 0:00:00.x to 23:59:59.y, where x
and y depend upon the precision.

RAML `type: time-only`

### Edm.DateTimeOffset

The DateTimeOffset type represents date and time as an offset in minutes from GMT, with values
ranging from 12:00:00 midnight, January 1, 1753 A.D. through 11:59:59 P.M, December 9999 A.D.

RAML `type: datetime`
---

# APIkit for OData v2 Module Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Integrate APIkit for OData v2 with APIkit for REST by declaring the Maven dependencies, namespaces,
and XML schema locations in this topic. Customize generated EntitySet names and router behavior from
your RAML types when you need endpoints that differ from default pluralization.

## Dependency Information

Declare the APIkit for OData v2 extension with the APIkit for REST module dependency:

<dependency>
    <groupId>org.mule.modules</groupId>
    <artifactId>mule-module-apikit-odata</artifactId>
    <version>2.2.0</version> **(1)**
    <classifier>mule-plugin</classifier>
</dependency>
<dependency>
    <groupId>org.mule.modules</groupId>
    <artifactId>mule-apikit-module</artifactId>
    <version>1.8.3</version> **(1)**
    <classifier>mule-plugin</classifier>
</dependency>

<table><tbody><tr><td><i data-value="1"></i><b>1</b></td><td>The tooling determines the appropriate version of each dependency when scaffolding to guarantee compatibility. Manually setting dependency versions produces issues. See the <a href="https://docs.mulesoft.com/release-notes/apikit/apikit-release-notes">APIkit release notes</a> before upgrading each component to the latest version.</td></tr></tbody></table>

> [!NOTE] Update the Anypoint Studio plugin to enable the tooling to determine the appropriate
> dependency combination.

## Namespace and Schema

xmlns:apikit="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/mule-apikit"
xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/mule-apikit
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/mule-apikit/current/mule-apikit.xsd"

## Customizing EntitySet Names

APIkit for OData v2 pluralizes entity type names automatically to create **EntitySet** endpoints
(for example, `Person` becomes `Persons`). For APIkit for OData 2.3.5 and later, override this
behavior by using these annotations in your RAML type definitions:

- **(odata.entitySet)**
- **(odata.entitySetEnabled)**

With these annotations, you control the **EntitySet** name exposed in metadata and in OData API URLs
and align endpoints with your domain (for example, change `Person` to `People`).

### Annotation Reference

Apply these annotations at the entity type level in your RAML library (for example, in
`odataLibrary.raml`):

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Annotation</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p><code>(odata.entitySet)</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Custom name for the entity set. Used only if <code>(odata.entitySetEnabled)</code> is <code>true</code> and the value is non-empty.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>(odata.entitySetEnabled)</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>true</code>, uses the custom name from <code>(odata.entitySet)</code>. If <code>false</code> or omitted, default pluralization is used for backward compatibility.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Feature Flag: Enabling Custom EntitySet Names

The `(odata.entitySet)` and `(odata.entitySetEnabled)` entity annotations affect metadata generation
and OData API behavior as follows:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Case</th><th><code>(odata.entitySetEnabled)</code></th><th><code>(odata.entitySet)</code></th><th>Effective EntitySet Name</th><th>Metadata and OData API Behavior</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>1</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>false</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Present</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Default pluralization (based on entity type)</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Default EntitySet</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>2</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>false</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Not present</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Default pluralization (based on entity type)</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Default EntitySet</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>3</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Not defined or present</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Present</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Default pluralization (based on entity type)</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Default EntitySet</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>4</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Not defined or present</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Not present</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Default pluralization (based on entity type)</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Default EntitySet</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>5</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>true</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Present (non-empty)</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Custom name from annotation</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Custom EntitySet; metadata and APIs use the custom name</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>6</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>true</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Not present or empty</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Default pluralization (based on entity type)</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Default EntitySet</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

> [!NOTE] Custom names apply if `entitySetEnabled` is `true` and `entitySet` is provided with a
> non-empty value. If not, APIkit for OData v2 uses default pluralization.

### Example: Custom EntitySet Name in RAML

This example defines a `Person` entity and exposes it as the `EntitySet` `People`:

```raml
#%RAML 1.0 Library
uses:
  odata: libraries/odataLibrary.raml

types:
  Person:
    (odata.remote): Person
    (odata.entitySet): People
    (odata.entitySetEnabled): true
    properties:
      ID:
        type: integer
        required: true
        (odata.key): true
      Name:
        type: string
        required: true
      Price:
        type: number
```

The `EntitySet` appears as `People` in `$metadata` and in OData API URLs (for example,
`/api/odata.svc/People`) instead of the default `Persons`.

### Backward Compatibility

Existing flows and APIs don√¢¬Ä¬ôt change unless you explicitly enable the feature. APIkit for OData v2
ignores custom `EntitySet` annotations unless `(odata.entitySetEnabled)` is set to `true`. If the
flag is `false` or missing, APIkit for OData v2 always uses default pluralization based on the
entity type, even if `(odata.entitySet)` is present. This ensures full backward compatibility with
existing APIkit for OData v2 applications.

## See Also

- [APIkit for REST Module Reference](apikit-4-xml-reference)
---

# APIkit for OData v4 Error Handling Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

APIkit for OData v4 maps routing, parsing, serialization, and expansion failures to typed
`APIKIT-ODATA` errors you handle in Mule. Use the error catalog and scaffolded handler in this
reference to interpret failures and return consistent OData error payloads.

## Error Types

APIkit for OData v4 defines error types for common OData processing failures:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Error Type</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>APIKIT-ODATA:NOT_IMPLEMENTED</p></td><td><p>Requested service, feature, or operation not implemented</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>APIKIT-ODATA:MALFORMED_URI</p></td><td><p>Invalid request URI</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>APIKIT-ODATA:MALFORMED_SEARCH</p></td><td><p>Invalid request <code>$search</code> expression in URI</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>APIKIT-ODATA:SERIALIZATION</p></td><td><p>Generic error for serialization issues</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>APIKIT-ODATA:DESERIALIZATION</p></td><td><p>Generic error for deserialization issues</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>APIKIT-ODATA:TRANSFORMATION</p></td><td><p>Generic error for issues related to URI to SQL transformations</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>APIKIT-ODATA:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR</p></td><td><p>Generic error for internal server issues</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>APIKIT-ODATA:NOT_FOUND</p></td><td><p>Generic error for not found</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>APIKIT-ODATA:EXPANSION</p></td><td><p>Generic error for issues during expansion</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Error Handling

APIkit for OData v4 scaffolder generates an error handler with a single `On Error Propagate` catch
that serves as a generalized error formatter.

```xml
...
    <error-handler name="Error_Handler">
      <on-error-propagate name="On_Error_Propagate" enableNotifications="true" logException="true" type="ANY">
        <ee:transform>
          <ee:message>
            <ee:set-payload><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/json
---
{
	error: {
		code: error.errorMessage.payload.code default "UNKNOWN",
		message: error.errorMessage.payload.message default error.description,
		target: error.errorMessage.payload.target,
		details: error.errorMessage.payload.details default [{code: "UNKNOWN", message: error.detailedDescription, target: null}],
		innererror: error.errorMessage.payload.innerError default error.childErrors
	}
}]]></ee:set-payload>
          </ee:message>
          <ee:variables>
            <ee:set-variable variableName="statusCode"><![CDATA[error.errorMessage.payload.statusCode default 500]]></ee:set-variable>
          </ee:variables>
        </ee:transform>
      </on-error-propagate>
    </error-handler>
...
```

## See Also

- [Error Handlers](../../mule-runtime/latest/error-handling)
---

# OData $expand in APIkit for OData v4

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

The OData `$expand` system query option returns related entities inline so clients avoid extra round
trips and smaller payloads on metered or high-latency networks. APIkit for OData v4 adds automatic
routing for common `$expand` scenarios from module 1.3.0 onward; you configure listeners and
optional generic expansion so existing flows can satisfy expanded queries. See the
[OASIS URL conventions](https://docs.oasis-open.org/odata/odata/v4.01/odata-v4.01-part2-url-conventions.html)
for full `$expand` syntax.

## Before You Begin

Prerequisites for using `$expand` include:

- APIkit for OData Module (version 1.3.0)
- A `<request-entity-collection-listener/>` implementation for each expandable entity
- Support for the `in` filter on the `<request-entity-collection-listener/>` flows

## `$expand` Feature Example

In this example:

- The `Employee` entity has a related `Manager` entity.
- The `Employees` entity set is a collection of `Employee` entities.
- The `Managers` entity set is a collection of `Manager` entities.
- The API location is `https://example.com/api/`.

Given this setup, the following requests retrieve employees and their managers:

- Use `GET https://example.com/api/Employees` to retrieve a list of employees.
- Gather the `Manager` IDs through the `ReferentialConstraint` of their relationship.
- Use `GET https://example.com/api/Managers?$filter=ManagerID in (id1, id2, ..., idN)` to retrieve a
  list of related managers.
- Write a small program to merge the lists based on join criteria.

If you use the `$expand` feature, the request to retrieve a list of employees with their `Manager`
navigation property expanded is `GET https://example.com/api/Employees?$expand=Manager`.

## The `generic expand` Feature

Reuse existing flow implementations as a starting point for expanding requests by using the
`generic expand` feature, which enables you to focus on the relevant data retrieval operations while
the module performs the necessary steps to ensure that the `$expand` fields are complete.

An application with the `generic expand` feature enabled works like any other OData Mule
application. However, during the flow execution, once the module retrieves information for the main
entity set, it performs additional internal requests to gather the information for any `expand`
field.

You can configure how the software handles errors during the expansion operation:

- Fails the request with an error.
- Replaces the failed expansion with a `null` value.

## The `generic expand` Operation

The `generic expand` feature dispatches additional queries to the application when the original flow
completes. Because this is done based on the application `EDM`, you must make extra annotations to
add enough context to the module:

- The `EntitySet` where the expansion takes place has a `NavigationPropertyBinding` for every
  expandable `NavigationProperty` of its `Entity`.

  This is used to select which `<entity-collection-source/>` is executed when expanding that
  property.

- Each expandable `NavigationProperty` has a `ReferentialConstraint` attached.

  Internal requests use `ReferentialConstraint` when matching the value of `Property` against the
  value of `ReferencedProperty`.

### Flow Execution

1.  The main flow is executed and completed resulting in a collection of `entities`.
2.  The OData module gathers all the properties that need expansion.
3.  For each `NavigationProperty`:
    1.  Get the `source property` from its `ReferentialConstraint`.
    2.  Get the `referenced property` from its `ReferentialConstraint`.
    3.  Get the `destination entity set` from its `NavigationPropertyBinding`.
    4.  For each of the `entities` get the resolved `source property`. Add these values to a
        `source property list`.
    5.  Execute the internal request by invoking the `<entity-collection-source/>` for
        `destination entity set` with `$filter=referenced property` in `(√¢¬Ä¬¶√¢¬Ä¬ãsource property list)`.

### Error Handling and Error Propagation

Error handling for OData v4 applications is local to each flow, whether you use `generic expansion`
or not. Error handling code for a given `EntitySet` belongs to the flow implementations for that
entity set, both for normal requests and for the internal requests observed during expansion
processing.

Handling errors during the expansion process is not supported, because the expansion process runs
after the execution of the flow finishes. There are two options available for handling errors:

- **Ignore errors during expansion:** Errors during generic expansion are ignored.  
  By default, the expansion of collections and single objects are resolved to empty lists and `null`
  values respectively.
- **Propagate errors during expansion:** Errors arise during generic expansion.  
  When an error reaches the OData v4 router, the original request is answered with an error
  response.

You can configure error handling **per-source** on the **Capabilities** tab.

### Limitations

- Only the first `ReferentialConstraint` is considered.

  Navigation properties that use compound keys are not supported.

- Expansion is done `field-by-field` instead of `EntitySet-by-EntitySet`.

  This can cause more internal requests than necessary.

- The expanded entities always have an `<entity-collection-source/>` implementation.

  Expansion using `<entity-source/>` implementations are not executed due to performance concerns.

## Implementation Guidelines

To implement the `generic expansion` feature, you must annotate each source with the types that it
can expand. Enabling `$expand` without restrictions enables API clients to write complex queries,
which can create performance issues.

If you implement `$expand` manually for some relationships, leave these relationships out of the
list on the **Expandable Navigation Properties** option. The navigation properties that don√¢¬Ä¬ôt use
the `generic expansion` feature need manual implementations in the flow.

### Implementation Checklist

1.  Verify that your CSDL file and Mule application meet the expected requirements:
    - Every `NavigationProperty` has one `ReferentialConstraint`.
    - Every `EntitySet` has one `NavigationPropertyBinding` for each `NavigationProperty` of its
      entity type.
    - Every `<entity-collection-source/>` supports the `in` operator for the `$filter` system query
      option.

2.  For each source for which you enable `generic expand` support:
    1.  Open the **Capabilities** tab.
    2.  Edit inline the **Expandable navigation properties** field.
    3.  Add the name of each navigation property to be expandable by using `generic expand`.
    4.  Depending on your use case, select **Ignore errors on expand** to avoid errors during the
        generic expansion and to generate errors on the main flow.

3.  Verify that your existing flows support the `$expand` feature.

## See Also

- [APIkit for OData v4 Module Reference](apikit-odatav4-extension-reference)
- [APIkit for OData v4 Error Handling Reference](apikit-odatav4-error-handling-reference)
---

# APIkit for OData v4 Module Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Configure APIkit for OData v4 in Mule with the Maven dependency, `apikit-odata` namespace, and
schema location listed in this reference. Later sections document `Config`, operations, and
listeners you use when you scaffold or extend OData v4 APIs.

## Dependency Information

<dependency>
  <groupId>org.mule.modules</groupId>
  <artifactId>mule-odata-module</artifactId>
  <version>LATEST\_VERSION</version> **(1)**
  <classifier>mule-plugin</classifier>
</dependency>

<table><tbody><tr><td><i data-value="1"></i><b>1</b></td><td>Check <a href="https://docs.mulesoft.com/release-notes/apikit/apikit-release-notes">APIkit release notes</a> to get the <strong>LATEST_VERSION</strong>.</td></tr></tbody></table>

## Namespace and Schema

xmlns:apikit-odata="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/apikit-odata"
xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/apikit-odata
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/apikit-odata/current/mule-apikit-odata.xsd"

## Configurations

### Config

Default configuration

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Name</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Api Definition</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>API definition</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Name</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Identifier of this element used to reference it in other components</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Operations

- [Route](#route)
- [Serialize Entity](#serialize-entity)
- [Serialize Entity Collection](#serialize-entity-collection)
- [Transform to SQL Select](#transform-to-sql-select)

- [RequestEntityListener](#on-entity-request)

## Operations

## Route

`<apikit-odata:route>`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Configuration</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Content</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The content to be routed.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Route correlation flows</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>true</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Output MIME outputs</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Config Ref</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Streaming Strategy</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-stream">RepeatableInMemoryStream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-stream">RepeatableFileStoreStream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configure if repeatable streams and their behavior are used</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Method</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>GET</p></li><li><p>POST</p></li><li><p>PUT</p></li><li><p>PATCH</p></li><li><p>DELETE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>HTTP request method name</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Masked Request Path</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>OData request path, relative to listener</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Scheme</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>HTTP</p></li><li><p>HTTPS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>HTTP request scheme</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Host</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Request host</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Listener Path</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Listener path ending with "*"</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Http Headers</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>HTTP request headers</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[attributes.headers]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Query String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>HTTP query string</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[attributes.queryString]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Target Variable</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of a variable on which the operation output is placed</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Target Value</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that is evaluated against the operation output and the outcome of that expression is stored in the target variable</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes Type</strong></p></td><td><p><a href="#route-output-attributes">RouteOutputAttributes</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#config)

### Throws

- APIKIT-ODATA:DESERIALIZATION
- APIKIT-ODATA:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- APIKIT-ODATA:MALFORMED_SEARCH
- APIKIT-ODATA:MALFORMED_URI
- APIKIT-ODATA:NOT_FOUND
- APIKIT-ODATA:NOT_IMPLEMENTED
- APIKIT-ODATA:SERIALIZATION
- APIKIT-ODATA:TRANSFORMATION
- APIKIT-ODATA:EXPANSION

## Serialize Entity

`<apikit-odata:serialize-entity>`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Configuration</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Inbound Content</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The binary content to serialize.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Output MIME Type</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Output Encoding</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encoding of the payload that this operation outputs</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Config Ref</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Streaming Strategy</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-stream">RepeatableInMemoryStream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-stream">RepeatableFileStoreStream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configure if repeatable streams and their behavior are used</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Method</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>GET</p></li><li><p>POST</p></li><li><p>PUT</p></li><li><p>PATCH</p></li><li><p>DELETE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>HTTP Method</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Path</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path to Entity Type</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Target Variable</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of a variable on which the operation output is placed</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Target Value</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that is evaluated against the operation output and the outcome of that expression is stored in the target variable</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><p>Binary</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#config)

## Serialize Entity Collection

`<apikit-odata:serialize-entity-collection>`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Configuration</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Inbound Content</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The binary content to serialize.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Output MIME Type</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Output Encoding</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encoding of the payload that this operation outputs</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Config Ref</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Streaming Strategy</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-stream">RepeatableInMemoryStream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-stream">RepeatableFileStoreStream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configure if repeatable streams and their behavior are used</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Method</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>GET</p></li><li><p>POST</p></li><li><p>PUT</p></li><li><p>PATCH</p></li><li><p>DELETE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>HTTP Method</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Path</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path to Entity Type</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Target Variable</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of a variable on which the operation output is placed</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Target Value</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that is evaluated against the operation output and the outcome of that expression is stored in the target variable</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Transform to SQL Select

`<apikit-odata:transform-to-sql-select>`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Config Ref</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Configuration</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Database Schema</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Database schema name to which the entity set belongs. If not empty, it√¢¬Ä¬ôs prefixed to the table name in the <code>WHERE</code> clause</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Entity Set Name</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Table name in the database for the requested entity set</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Entity Type Fields</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>List of property fields from the Entity type</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Entity Type Keys</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Map of entity key names and values</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Entity Type Name</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Entity Data Model specifies the entity type name</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Page Size</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The number of records to retrieve.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>searchFieldsAsType</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Data type to which fields are type-casted for the <code>$search</code> operation. If empty, no type-casting is performed</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Search Transformation Enabled</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Enabling <code>$search</code> transformation to <code>WHERE</code> clause</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>true</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Sql Identifiers Delimiter</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>DOUBLE_QUOTES</p></li><li><p>BRACKETS</p></li><li><p>BACK_TICKS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Option to delimit identifiers</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>DOUBLE_QUOTES</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Sql Limit Rows</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>LIMIT</p></li><li><p>FETCH_NEXT_ROWS_ONLY</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Option to limit the number of rows</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>LIMIT</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Strict Search Term Match</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Enabling full term match for <code>$search</code>. If enabled, terms in <code>WHERE</code> clause aren√¢¬Ä¬ôt enclosed between '%'.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>false</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Streaming Strategy</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-stream">RepeatableInMemoryStream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-stream">RepeatableFileStoreStream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configure if repeatable streams and their behavior are used</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>System Query Options</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>System Query Options map. The grammar and syntax rules for system query options are defined in [OData-ABNF]. To simplify usability, the initial dollar sign is removed to avoid escaping within DataWeave. For example, to access System Query Option, <code>$skip</code> uses the following expression #[attributes.systemQueryOptions.'skip']</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[attributes.systemQueryOptions]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Target Variable</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of a variable on which the operation output is placed</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Target Value</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that is evaluated against the operation output and the outcome of that expression is stored in the target variable</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><p>Binary</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes Type</strong></p></td><td><p>* <a href="#sql-transformer-output-attributes">SqlTransformerOutputAttributes</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#config)

### Throws

- APIKIT-ODATA:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- APIKIT-ODATA:MALFORMED_SEARCH
- APIKIT-ODATA:MALFORMED_URI
- APIKIT-ODATA:NOT_IMPLEMENTED
- APIKIT-ODATA:TRANSFORMATION

## Sources

## On Entity Collection Request

`<apikit-odata:request-entity-collection-listener>`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Configuration</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Output MIME Type</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Output Encoding</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encoding of the payload that this operation outputs</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Config Ref</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Primary Node Only</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specify if this source executes only on the primary node when running in Cluster</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>false</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Streaming Strategy</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-stream">RepeatableInMemoryStream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-stream">RepeatableFileStoreStream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configure if repeatable streams and their behavior are used</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Redelivery Policy</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#redelivery-policy">RedeliveryPolicy</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Policy for processing the redelivery of the same message</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Method</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>GET</p></li><li><p>POST</p></li><li><p>PUT</p></li><li><p>PATCH</p></li><li><p>DELETE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>HTTP Method</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Path</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path to Entity Type</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Collection Success Response</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#raw-entity-collection-success-response">RawEntityCollectionSuccessResponse</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#entity-collection-success-response">EntityCollectionSuccessResponse</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Supported System Query Options - <code>$expand</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Enabling support for <code>$expand</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>true</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Ignore errors on expansion enabled</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Disallowing errors from the flows that generic expansion calls to propagate to this flow. When <code>true</code>, failed expansions render as <code>null</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>false</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Expandable navigation properties</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>List&lt;String&gt;</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>List of types that can be generically expanded from this flow.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>[]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes Type</strong></p></td><td><p><a href="#listener-request-attributes">ListenerRequestAttributes</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>
