## On Entity Request

`<apikit-odata:request-entity-listener>`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Configuration</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Output MIME Type</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Output Encoding</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encoding of the payload that this operation outputs</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Config Ref</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Primary Node Only</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If this source must only be executed on the primary node when running in Cluster</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>false</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Streaming Strategy</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-stream">RepeatableInMemoryStream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-stream">RepeatableFileStoreStream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configure if repeatable streams and their behavior are used</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Redelivery Policy</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#redelivery-policy">RedeliveryPolicy</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Policy for processing the redelivery of the same message</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Method</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>GET</p></li><li><p>POST</p></li><li><p>PUT</p></li><li><p>PATCH</p></li><li><p>DELETE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>HTTP Method</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Path</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path to Entity Type</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Supported System Query Options - <code>$expand</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Enabling support for <code>$expand</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>true</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Ignore errors on expansion enabled</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Disallowing errors from the flows that generic expansion calls to propagate to this flow. When <code>true</code>, failed expansions render as <code>null</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>false</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Expandable navigation properties</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>List&lt;String&gt;</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>List of types that can be generically expanded from this flow</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>[]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes Type</strong></p></td><td><p><a href="#listener-request-attributes">ListenerRequestAttributes</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#config)

## Types

### Listener Request Attributes

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Entity Set Name</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the requested entity set.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[attributes.entitySetName]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Odata Http Attributes</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#odata-http-attributes">ODataHttpAttributes</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>HTTP attributes of the OData request.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[attributes.odataHttpAttributes]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Odata Request Attributes</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#odata-request-attributes">ODataRequestAttributes</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>OData-specific attributes of the OData request.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[attributes.odataRequestAttributes]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### OData Http Attributes

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Headers</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An object containing the HTTP headers of the incoming request. This allows the router to access the request headers.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[attributes.headers]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Method</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>GET</p></li><li><p>POST</p></li><li><p>PUT</p></li><li><p>PATCH</p></li><li><p>DELETE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The HTTP method of the OData request.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Raw Base Uri</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Base URI of the OData request</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[attributes.baseUri]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Raw Odata Path</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Raw OData path of the OData request</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[attributes.rawODataPath]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Raw Query Path</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Raw query path of the OData request</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[attributes.rawQueryPath]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Raw Request Uri</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Raw request URI of the OData request</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[attributes.rawRequestUri]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Scheme</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>HTTP</p></li><li><p>HTTPS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### OData Request Attributes

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Custom Query Options</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Custom query options of the OData request</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[attributes.customQueryOptions]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Entity Type Fields</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Entity-type fields of the OData request</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[attributes.entityTypeFields]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Entity Type Keys</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Entity-type keys of the OData request</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[attributes.entityTypeKeys]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Entity Type Name</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Entity-type name of the OData request</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[attributes.entityTypeName]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>System Query Options</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>System query options of the OData request</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[attributes.systemQueryOptions]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable in Memory Stream

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Initial Buffer Size</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Amount of memory allocated to consume the stream and provide random access to it. If the stream has more data than the buffer capacity, it expands according to the <code>bufferSizeIncrement</code> attribute, with an upper limit of <code>maxInMemorySize</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Buffer Size Increment</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expansion of the buffer size if exceeding its initial size. If you set a value of zero or lower, the buffer doesn√¢¬Ä¬ôt expand and raises a <code>STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED</code> error when the buffer gets full.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Max Buffer Size</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The maximum amount of memory for the buffer to use. When memory usage exceeds this limit, a <code>STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED</code> error is raised. A value lower or equal to zero means no limit.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Buffer Unit</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Unit to express these attributes</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable File Store Stream

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>In Memory Size</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum memory that the stream uses to keep data in memory. If more memory is consumed, the stream starts to buffer the content on the disk</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Buffer Unit</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Unit to express <code>maxInMemorySize</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>KB</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Redelivery Policy

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Max Redelivery Count</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum number of times a message can be redelivered and processed unsuccessfully before triggering process-failed-message</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Message Digest Algorithm</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Secure hashing algorithm (default: SHA-256)</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>SHA-256</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Message Identifier</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#redelivery-policy-message-identifier">RedeliveryPolicyMessageIdentifier</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Strategy to identify the messages</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Object Store</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ObjectStore</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The object store where the redelivery counter for each message is stored</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Use Secure Hash</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether to use a secure hash algorithm to identify a redelivered message</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Id Expression</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>One or more expressions that determine when a message is redelivered. You can set this property if <code>useSecureHash</code> is <code>false</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>none</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Redelivery Policy Message Identifier

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Use Secure Hash</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether to use a secure hash algorithm to identify a redelivered message</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Id Expression</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>One or more expressions that determine when a message is redelivered. You can set this property if <code>useSecureHash</code> is <code>false</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Route Output Attributes

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Headers</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An object representing the HTTP headers to be included in the response.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[attributes.headers]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Status Code</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The HTTP status code to be returned in the response.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### SQL Transformer Output Attributes

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Query Parameters</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An object representing the query parameters to be included in the response.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Raw Entity Success Response

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Raw Content</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Raw response content of the response message</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Entity Success Response

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Body Content</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Body of the response message</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Raw Entity Collection Success Response

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Raw Content</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Raw response message</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Entity Collection Success Response

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Body Content</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Body of the response message</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Serialization Parameters</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#serialization-parameters">SerializationParameters</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Set of serialization-related parameters.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Serialization Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Count</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The number of entities.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Page Size</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The number of entities per page.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Token</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The token to retrieve the next page.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>None</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

- [APIkit for OData v4 Error Handling Reference](apikit-odatav4-error-handling-reference)
---

# Transforming a RAML OData v2 API to a CSDL OData v4 API

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Migrate from APIkit for OData v2 to OData v4 by reusing your RAML entity model: Studio converts
`odata.raml` into `odata.csdl.xml` (Common Schema Definition Language) so you can scaffold OData v4
flows from the same types. The new file lands under the `api` folder next to your existing assets.

In Studio, right-click your `odata.raml` file and select **Mule** > **Generate CSDL file**.

![*Generate CSDL file* option highlighted in the Mule section when right-clicking the OData file in the Package Explorer](_images/generate-csdl-file.png)

APIkit generates an `odata.csdl.xml` file in the **api** folder.

![*odata.csdl.xml* new file highlighted in the *api* folder, in the *Package Explorer*, and the XML code of the *odata.csdl.xml* file after its transformation](_images/csdl-xml.png)
---

# Transforming an OData Request to a SQL Query

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

The `Transform to SQL Select` operation turns an inbound OData `GET` into an SQL `SELECT` with bound
parameters you pass to the Database Connector. Pair it with the connector√¢¬Ä¬ôs `select` operation to
expose relational data through OData v4 without hand-writing SQL for every query. The message
payload is the prepared statement text; attributes carry the parameter map the database driver
expects.

![*Transform to SQL select* element and *Entity set name* field highlighted](_images/transform-to-sql-transformer.png)

The output payload is a prepared statement and the attributes are a map of prepared statement
parameters.

![*Select database* and *Input Parameters* field highlighted](_images/add-db-transform-to-sql.png)

You can use this operation with the Database connector to expose databases that use OData V4.

![*Serialized entity* element and the *Inbound content* field highlighted](_images/serialize-transform-to-sql.png)

APIkit supports these OData request elements:

- System query options:
  - $filter
  - $select
  - $search
  - $orderby
  - $skip
  - $top

- Single entity
- Entity relation
- Server-side pagination

APIkit does not support:

- Parameter Aliases
- Built-in Filter Operations:
  - has
  - divby

- Built-in Query Functions, all except:
  - contains
  - startswith
  - endwith

- $apply
- $compute
- $expand

## Transform to SQL Select Operation in OData V4

The OData protocol defines a series of conventions that enable clients to query identified resources
by using URLs. The list of queryable entities, their fields, and the relationships between them are
defined in a data model by using HTTP messages. OData shares some similarities with Java database
connectivity (JDBC). The `transform-to-sql-select` operation uses such similarities to help you
create SQL queries for the resources. The operation results in a message that has a prepared
statement with the SQL `select` sentence as the payload and the parameters name and values map as
the attributes.

## Implement the Transform to SQL Select Operation by Using APIkit for OData V4

You can implement the transform to SQL operation by using the
`<apikit-odata:transform-to-sql-select>` XML element, which has the following parameters:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter name</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Display name</p></td><td><p>Name of the operation.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Module configuration</p></td><td><p>Name of the related-module configuration.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Entity set name</p></td><td><p>Name of the entity set to fetch #[attributes.entitySetName].</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page size</p></td><td><p>Number that defines the amount of results per page during server-side pagination.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Entity type name</p></td><td><p>String obtained from the OData request by using #[attributes.odataRequestAttributes], which the entity data model specifies.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Entity type fields</p></td><td><p>Array of strings obtained from the OData request by using #[attributes.odataRequestAttributes], which provides the list of property fields obtained from the entity type.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Entity type keys</p></td><td><p>Object obtained from the OData request by using #[attributes.odataRequestAttributes], which provides a map of entity key names and values.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>System query options</p></td><td><p>Parameter obtained from the OData request by using #[attributes.odataRequestAttributes].</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Search transformation enabled</p></td><td><p>If <code>true</code>, this option enables the $search transformation option for a WHERE clause.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Strict search term match</p></td><td><p>If <code>true</code>, this option enables full term matching for $search. If enabled, terms in the WHERE clause aren√¢¬Ä¬ôt enclosed between '%'.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>SQL identifiers delimiter</p></td><td><div><div><p>Option that delimits identifiers in the generated SQL statement (Options: DOUBLE_QUOTES, BRACKETS, and BACK_TICKS).</p></div><div><p>Examples:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>DOUBLE_QUOTES: <code>SELECT "TrackId", "Name" FROM "Tracks"</code></p></li><li><p>BRACKETS: <code>SELECT [TrackId], [Name] FROM [Tracks]</code></p></li><li><p>BACK_TICKS: <code>SELECT `TrackId`, `Name` FROM `Tracks`</code></p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>SQL limit rows</p></td><td><div><div><p>Option that limits the numbers of rows (Options: LIMIT and FETCH_NEXT_ROWS_ONLY).</p></div><div><p>Examples:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>LIMIT: <code>SELECT OrderID, ShippedDate, ShipCountry FROM Orders LIMIT :parameter1</code></p></li><li><p>FETCH_NEXT_ROWS_ONLY: <code>SELECT OrderID, ShippedDate, ShipCountry FROM Orders ORDER BY OrderID ASC OFFSET :parameter2 ROWS FETCH NEXT :parameter1 ROWS ONLY</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p><code>ORDER BY</code> is automatically included if not present in the request by using the entity key defined in CSDL.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Date Transform Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Values provided, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>String: <code>Date</code> or <code>DateTimeOffset</code> parameters pass as <code>string</code> (default value).</p></li><li><p>java.time - LocalDate and LocalDateTime: <code>Date</code> or <code>DateTimeOffset</code> parameters pass as objects of <code>java.time</code> package classes <code>LocalDate</code> and <code>LocalDateTime</code>. This option enables most DB drivers -such as MySQL- to identify and process <code>Date/DateTime</code> parameters and return the correct result.</p></li><li><p>java.time - LocalDate and OffsetDateTime: <code>Date</code> or <code>DateTimeOffset</code> parameters pass as objects of <code>java.time</code> package classes <code>LocalDate</code> and <code>OffsetDateTime</code>. This option enables most DB drivers -such as Oracle- to identify and process <code>Date</code>, <code>Timestamp</code>, and <code>Timestamp with Time Zone</code> parameters and return the correct result.</p></li></ul></div><div><p>This operation takes the <code>Date/DateTime</code> values as strings by default. Select one of the <code>java.time object</code> options to output it as a corresponding <code>Date</code> or <code>DateTime</code> object in the <code>java.time</code> package.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## $filter Expressions in OData V4

√¢¬Ä¬ã√¢¬Ä¬ãThe OData `$filter` expression uses comparison, logical, arithmetic, and grouping operators. For a
list of operators and their meaning, see
[the OData4 Specification](https://docs.oasis-open.org/odata/odata/v4.01/cs01/part1-protocol/odata-v4.01-cs01-part1-protocol.html).

APIkit does not support the `has` and `divby` operators.

## Expressions Supported by Transform to SQL Select

For the filtering expressions that OData supports, see the
[specification](https://docs.oasis-open.org/odata/odata/v4.01/cs01/part1-protocol/odata-v4.01-cs01-part1-protocol.html).

The `transform-to-sql-select` operation supports the following subset of unary and binary operators,
and the following method operators.

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>OData Operator</th><th>SQL Operator</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>add</p></td><td><p>+</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>and</p></td><td><p>AND</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>div</p></td><td><p>/</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>eq</p></td><td><p>=</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>ge</p></td><td><p>&gt;=</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>gt</p></td><td><p>&gt;</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>le</p></td><td><p>=&lt;</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>lt</p></td><td><p>&lt;</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>mod</p></td><td><p>%</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>mul</p></td><td><p>*</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>ne</p></td><td><p>&lt;&gt;</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>or</p></td><td><p>OR</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>sub</p></td><td><p>-</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>not</p></td><td><p>NOT</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>-</p></td><td><p>-</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>contains</p></td><td><p>LIKE %value%</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>startswith</p></td><td><p>LIKE value%</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>endswith</p></td><td><p>LIKE %value</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## $search Support

This operation builds a `WHERE` clause based on the `$search` expression that the system query
option provides. The implementation selects all of the elements that have property values that match
the condition.

By default, a term matches if the field value contains the term. If the `strict search term match`
is set to `true` in the configuration, the `search` operation requires an exact match between the
field value and the search term.

The `search transformation enabled` and `strict search term match` parameters define the `$search`
feature.

## See Also

- [APIkit for OData v4 Module Reference](apikit-odatav4-extension-reference)
- [APIkit for OData v4 Error Handling Reference](apikit-odatav4-error-handling-reference)
---

- [APIkit 4.0 Release Notes](../../release-notes/apikit/apikit-4.0-release-notes)

# Configuring the Response Headers and Status Code

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Map APIkit for REST outbound headers and HTTP status values to variables the HTTP Listener writes
into each response. Configure `outboundHeadersMapName` and `httpStatusVarName` on `apikit:config` so
flows set `vars.outboundHeaders` and `vars.httpStatus` consistently across success and error paths.
The steps align listener response blocks with those variable names.

## Configure Variable Names

You can configure the default variables that the HTTP listener searches for by configuring these
properties in **APIkit Router**:

- `outboundHeadersMapName`

  Name of the variable that contains a headers map added in the response (`outboundHeaders` by
  default)

- `httpStatusVarName`

  Name of the variable that stores the status code to set in the response (`httpStatus` by default)

**APIkit Router** creates both variables and facilitates including custom HTTP headers and status
codes in the response.

```xml
<apikit:config  name="api-config"
                api="api.raml"
                outboundHeadersMapName="outboundHeaders"
                httpStatusVarName="httpStatus"/>
```

To modify the default names of these variables, update the default HTTP listener configuration to
align the variable names and the configuration.

```xml
<http:listener config-ref="api-httpListenerConfig" path="/api/*">
    <http:response
        statusCode="#[vars.httpStatus default 200]">
        <http:headers>#[vars.outboundHeaders default {}]</http:headers>
    </http:response>
    <http:error-response
        statusCode="#[vars.httpStatus default 500]">
        <http:body>#[payload]</http:body>
        <http:headers>#[vars.outboundHeaders default {}]</http:headers>
    </http:error-response>
</http:listener>
```

## Add a Header to a Response

Put the header to add in the `outboundHeaders` map, as shown in the following example:

```xml
<set-variable
    value='#[vars.outboundHeaders default {} ++ {newHeaderName: "Header value"}]'
    variableName="outboundHeaders"/>
```

## Set the Status Code of a Response

Use the following example as a reference to assign a specific value to the `httpStatus` variable:

```xml
<set-variable
    value="201"
    variableName="httpStatus"/>
```

## See Also

- [Sending HTTP responses when configuring HTTP listener source in Mule 4](https://docs.mulesoft.com/http-connector/1.7/http-listener-ref#sending-http-responses)
---

# Configure the Service URI

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Deployments behind a load balancer or API proxy often rewrite public URLs; APIkit for REST must emit
links and metadata that match what clients see. Set **Keep RAML/OAS base URI** (the `keepApiBaseUri`
attribute on `apikit:config`) so the base URI in your RAML or OpenAPI Specification stays aligned
with the gateway address.

Set `keepApiBaseUri` to `true` in XML:

```xml
<apikit:config  name="api-config"
                api="api.raml"
                outboundHeadersMapName="outboundHeaders"
                httpStatusVarName="httpStatus"
                keepApiBaseUri="true" />
```
---

# Building, Implementing, and Testing an OData V2 API

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Build an OData v2 REST API with APIkit for OData and the MySQL walkthrough: generate flows from RAML
types, connect to a sample database, and exercise HTTP and OData endpoints. The OData MySQL example
packages a working Maven project you import into Anypoint Studio.

## Before You Begin

Install this software to create and use an OData REST API with APIkit:

- OData Plugin
- Mule runtime engine 4.1.1 or later
- Anypoint Studio 7.1.4 or later

Additionally, you must download the [OData example entity data model](_attachments/odata.raml).

## Implement an OData v2 API Using the APIkit OData Extension

This section walks through implementing an OData-enabled REST API:

1.  In Studio, select **File** > **New** > **Mule Project**.
2.  In the **Project Name** field, type the name of your Mule project.
3.  Click **Finish**.
4.  Copy the `odata.raml` file you downloaded in the prerequisites section to the
    `/src/main/resources/api` directory in your project.
5.  In the project explorer, right-click `odata.raml` and select **Mule** > **Generate OData API
    from RAML Types**.

    ![A window highlighting the full path to the generate OData API from RAML types option.](_images/generate-odata.png)

6.  The OData extension generates the necessary files for your OData API implementation:

    ![Three files highlighted in the tree: api.xml in the Mule folder, and api.raml and odatalibrary.raml files in the resource folder.](_images/list-of-odata-files.png)

<table><tbody><tr><td><i data-value="1"></i><b>1</b></td><td>Mule configuration file with the flows for the API implementation</td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="2"></i><b>2</b></td><td>Generated RAML definition</td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="3"></i><b>3</b></td><td>Generated RAML libraries</td></tr></tbody></table>

7.  Add the necessary logic for your API implementation in the flows.

## OData MySQL Example

The OData MySQL example is a fully functional OData API packaged as a Maven project. In this
example, the data source, the app, and the service are local.

First, you install a MySQL database and load tables using a provided script. Next, you import the
compressed project into Studio, which includes a MySQL database driver. You can examine the flows to
see how to implement the endpoints you need by accessing the data. Then you run the project and call
the REST and OData services.

### Install and Populate a MySQL Database

1.  Install a MySQL database and launch MySQL.
2.  Download the MySQL project example
    [apikit-odata-example-main.jar](_attachments/apikit-odata-example-main.jar).
3.  Run the `example.sql` script located in `src/main/resources` using MySQL commands to load data
    into a database named `apikit-odata-example`.

### Implement the OData API

1.  In Studio, Select **File** > **Import**.
2.  Select **Anypoint Studio** > **Packaged mule application (.jar)** > **Next**.
3.  Browse for the `apikit-odata-example-main.jar` file and click **Finish**.
4.  In Studio, edit the `mule-configuration.properties` file in the `src/main/resources` folder, and
    set the following properties to access the local MySQL database:

    ```html
    ds.db.port=3306 ds.db.user=<your MySQL user name>
      ds.db.host=<your MySQL host name>
        ds.db.database=apikit-odata-example ds.db.password=<your MySQL password></your></your
    ></your>
    ```

5.  Run the API locally by right-clicking the project, and selecting **Run As** > **Mule
    Application**.

You can now access the REST and OData Service.

## Access the REST and OData Service

To run the API locally:

1.  Right-click the project, and select **Run As** > **Mule Application**.
2.  Access the REST and OData Service by using the following URLs:
    - REST API: `/api`
    - OData API: `/api/odata.svc`

      The following examples cover a few of the many REST calls and OData queries you can use.

### Retrieve a List of Customers

Call the REST API to retrieve the list of customers:

http://localhost:8081/api/customers

The response is:

```JSON
{
  "entries": [
    {
      "ContactName": "Maria Anders",
      "ContactTitle": "Sales Representative",
      "CompanyName": "Alfreds Futterkiste",
      "CustomerID": ""
    },
    {
      "ContactName": "Maria Anders",
      "ContactTitle": "Sales Representative",
      "CompanyName": "Alfreds Futterkiste",
      "CustomerID": "ALFKI"
    },
  ]
}
```

### Access a Description of the OData Service

Get information about the collections behind this service:

http://localhost:8081/api/odata.svc

The response is:

```xml
<service xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2007/app" xmlns:atom="http://www.w3.org/2005/Atom" xmlns:app="http://www.w3.org/2007/app" xml:base="http://localhost:8081">
  <workspace>
    <atom:title>Default</atom:title>
    <collection href="customers">
      <atom:title>customers</atom:title>
    </collection>
    <collection href="orders">
      <atom:title>orders</atom:title>
    </collection>
  </workspace>
</service>
```

### Get OData Service Metadata

The service metadata exposes the structure of OData service resources, operations, and EDM for a
given service.

Get the metadata for HTTP services:

http://localhost:8081/api/odata.svc/$metadata

The response aligns with the `odata.raml` EDM you used to build the HTTP Services API example:

```xml
<edmx:Edmx xmlns:edmx="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2007/06/edmx" Version="1.0">
<edmx:DataServices xmlns:m="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2007/08/dataservices/metadata" m:DataServiceVersion="2.0">
<Schema xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2008/09/edm" Namespace="odata2.namespace">
<EntityType Name="customers">
<Key>
<PropertyRef Name="CustomerID"/>
</Key>
<Property Name="CompanyName" Type="Edm.String" Nullable="true" MaxLength="40" Unicode="false"/>
<Property Name="ContactName" Type="Edm.String" Nullable="true" MaxLength="30" Unicode="false"/>
<Property Name="ContactTitle" Type="Edm.String" Nullable="true" MaxLength="30" Unicode="false"/>
<Property Name="CustomerID" Type="Edm.String" Nullable="false" MaxLength="5" Unicode="false"/>
</EntityType>
<EntityType Name="orders">
<Key>
<PropertyRef Name="OrderID"/>
<PropertyRef Name="ShipName"/>
</Key>
<Property Name="Freight" Type="Edm.Decimal" Nullable="true" Precision="3" Scale="3" Unicode="false"/>
...
```

### Create a Customer Entity Using OData Service

Create a POST request with the following body:

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" standalone="yes"?>
<entry xmlns:d="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2007/08/dataservices" xmlns:m="http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2007/08/dataservices/metadata" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2005/Atom">
<content type="application/xml">
    <m:properties>
    <d:CustomerID>BOTTM</d:CustomerID>
    <d:CompanyName>Bottom-Dollar Markets</d:CompanyName>
    <d:ContactName>Elizabeth Lincoln</d:ContactName>
    <d:ContactTitle>Accounting Manager</d:ContactTitle>
    </m:properties>
</content>
</entry>

The request must have the following headers:

Content-Type: application/xml Accept: application/xml

Call the REST API to create a customer entity:

http://localhost:8081/api/odata.svc/customers

### Query the Data Source

Issue OData queries to get the list of customers in XML and JSON format:

http://localhost:8081/api/odata.svc/customers
http://localhost:8081/api/odata.svc/customers?$format=json

Issue an OData query to get the tenth customer in the customer list:

http://localhost:8081/api/odata.svc/customers?$format=json&$top=1&$skip=10&$inlinecount=allpages

The response is:

```json
{
  "d": {
    "results": [
      {
        "__metadata": {
          "uri": "http:/localhost:8081/api/odata.svc/customers('BOTTM')",
          "type": "odata2.namespace.customers"
        },
        "CompanyName": "Bottom-Dollar Markets",
        "ContactName": "Elizabeth Lincoln",
        "ContactTitle": "Accounting Manager",
        "CustomerID": "BOTTM"
      }
    ],
    "__count": "98"
  }
}
```

## Implement Endpoints for an OData API

You can implement API endpoints such as the `get:/customers:config` and `get:/orders:config` flows
using Anypoint Studio. The output format of the flows must be JSON. For more information, see the
[complete functioning example](creating-an-odatav4-api-with-apikit#odata-mysql-example).

### Declare Input Variables for a GET

1.  Declare a variable for the APIkit OData Service variable that contains the fields of your entity
    in a list of strings (List<String>). For example:

    ```javascript
    var entityFields = vars.odata.fields;
    ```

2.  Declare a variable for the APIkit OData Service variable that filters into 'http.query.params'.
    For example:

    ```javascript
    var filters = attributes.queryParams;
    ```

3.  Declare a variable for the APIkit OData Service variable that contains the keys of your entity.
    For example:

    ```javascript
    var keys = vars.odata.keyNames;
    ```

4.  Declare a variable for the APIkit OData Service variable that contains the table name. For
    example:

    ```javascript
    var remoteEntityName = vars.odata.remoteEntityName;
    ```

### Declare Input Variables for a POST

1.  Declare a variable for the APIkit OData Service inbound property that contains the entity√¢¬Ä¬ôs
    name:

    ```javascript
    var remoteEntityName = vars.odata.remoteEntityName;
    ```

2.  Declare a variable for transforming your payload into something like this: { myKey1: 'myValue1',
    myKey2: 'myValue2'}.

    ```javascript
    var valuesFromPayload = {
      keys: payload pluck $$,
      values: payload pluck "'$'"
    }
    ```

3.  Declare two variables, and use 'joinBy' to transform your keys and values into
    comma-separated-values (CSV). For example:

    ```javascript
    var columns = ( (valuesFromPayload.keys map "`$`" ) joinBy ", ") // myKey1, myKey2
    var values = (valuesFromPayload.values joinBy ", ") // 'myValue1', 'myValue2'
    ```

### Format Output

The output for each flow must conform to the following format:

```xml
{
  "entries":
  [
    {<entry1>},
    {<entry2>},
    {<entryN>}
  ]
}
```

## See Also

- [APIkit for OData v2 Module Reference](apikit-odatav2-extension-reference)
- [Deploy to CloudHub](../../cloudhub/deploying-to-cloudhub)
---

# Building, Implementing, and Testing an OData V4 API

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Build an OData v4 API with APIkit for OData using CSDL metadata, scaffolding, and the
[OData MySQL example](#apikit-for-odata-4-implementation-example) to query MySQL over HTTP. The
tutorial covers project setup, generated flows, and running the packaged sample.

## Before You Begin

Install this software to create and use an OData REST API with APIkit:

- OData Plugin
- Mule runtime engine 4.3.0 or later
- Anypoint Studio 7.9.0 or later

Additionally, you must download the
[OData 4 implementation example](_attachments/apikit-odata4-example.jar).

## Scaffold an OData V4 API Using APIkit for OData

1.  In Studio, select **File** > **New** > **Mule Project**.
2.  In the **Project Name** field, type the name of your Mule project.
3.  Click **Finish**.
4.  In `src/main/resources/api`, create a new file with a `.csdl.xml` extension.

    For example, `odata-metadata.csdl.xml`:

    ```xml
    <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
    <edmx:Edmx Version="4.0" xmlns:edmx="http://docs.oasis-open.org/odata/ns/edmx">
      <edmx:DataServices>
        <Schema xmlns="http://docs.oasis-open.org/odata/ns/edm" Namespace="odata4.namespace">
          <EntityType Name="Customers"> (1)
            <Key> (2)
              <PropertyRef Name="CustomerID" />
            </Key>
            <Property Name="CompanyName" Type="Edm.String" MaxLength="40" Unicode="false" />
            <Property Name="ContactName" Type="Edm.String" MaxLength="30" Unicode="false" />
            <Property Name="ContactTitle" Type="Edm.String" MaxLength="30" Unicode="false" />
            <Property Name="CustomerID" Type="Edm.String" Nullable="false" MaxLength="5" Unicode="false" />
            <NavigationProperty Name="Orders" Type="Collection(odata4.namespace.Orders)"/>
          </EntityType>
          <EntityType Name="Orders">
            <Key>
              <PropertyRef Name="OrderID" />
              <PropertyRef Name="ShipName" />
            </Key>
            <Property Name="Freight" Type="Edm.Decimal" Precision="19" Scale="4" />
            <Property Name="OrderDate" Type="Edm.DateTimeOffset" Unicode="false" />
            <Property Name="OrderID" Type="Edm.Int32" Nullable="false" Unicode="false" />
            <Property Name="CustomerID" Type="Edm.String" MaxLength="5"/>
            <Property Name="Price" Type="Edm.Single" Unicode="false" />
            <Property Name="Priority" Type="Edm.Int16" Unicode="false" />
            <Property Name="ShipAddress" Type="Edm.String" MaxLength="60" Unicode="false" />
            <Property Name="ShipName" Type="Edm.String" Nullable="false" MaxLength="40" Unicode="false" />
            <NavigationProperty Name="Customer" Type="odata4.namespace.Customers">
            	<ReferentialConstraint Property="CustomerID" ReferencedProperty="CustomerID" />
            </NavigationProperty>
          </EntityType>
          <EntityContainer Name="OData4EntityContainer"> (3)
            <EntitySet Name="Customers" EntityType="odata4.namespace.Customers"> (4)
            	<NavigationPropertyBinding Path="Orders" Target="Orders"/>
            </EntitySet>
            <EntitySet Name="Orders" EntityType="odata4.namespace.Orders"/>
          </EntityContainer>
        </Schema>
      </edmx:DataServices>
    </edmx:Edmx>
    ```

    <table><tbody><tr><td><i data-value="1"></i><b>1</b></td><td><code>EntityType</code> defines the type and its properties.</td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="2"></i><b>2</b></td><td><code>key</code> defines which property or set of properties is the key for the entity.<div><p>For example, the entity set <code>Orders</code> comprises two keys: <code>OrderID</code> and <code>ShipName</code>.</p></div></td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="3"></i><b>3</b></td><td><code>EntityContainer</code> defines the sets of exposed entities.<div><p>In this example, <code>Orders</code> and <code>Customers</code>.</p></div></td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="4"></i><b>4</b></td><td><code>EntitySet</code> contains a name and EntityType.<div><p>You must define them in the <code>schema</code> namespace.</p></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

5.  In the **Package Explorer**, right-click your `odata-metadata.csdl.xml` file and select
    **Generate Mule OData 4 API**:

    ![The path to the Generate Mule OData 4 API option highlighted](_images/generate-odata-v4-api.png)

6.  APIkit generates the corresponding flows based on your `.csdl.xml` metadata file.

    ![The three flows created from the `csdl.xml` metadata file are highlighted.](_images/generated-odata-4-flow.png)

    <table><tbody><tr><td><i data-value="1"></i><b>1</b></td><td>The main flow receives messages and routes the operation to the correct handler.</td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="2"></i><b>2</b></td><td>Five flows with <code>GET</code>, <code>PUT</code>, <code>POST</code>, and <code>DELETE</code> operations for each entity.</td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="3"></i><b>3</b></td><td>A flow with a <code>GET</code> operation to retrieve the collection of entities.</td></tr></tbody></table>

## Handle OData System Query Options

Within each **On Entity Request** and **On Entity Collection Request** listener you can access a
`systemQueryOptions` attribute that maps to each OData query option (`$select`, `$orderby`,
`$count`, etc.). For example, to access the `$select` value you use the following expression:

```none
#[attributes.odataRequestAttributes.systemQueryOptions.select]
```

![odataRequestAttributes highlighted in the output tab, within the Mule message, under attributes](_images/odata-query-option-attributes.png)

## APIkit for OData 4 Implementation Example

1.  Download the full [apikit-odata4-example](_attachments/apikit-odata4-example.jar).
2.  Import the downloaded example in Studio.
3.  Run the SQL statements in the src/main/resources/example.sql file to create the database,
    tables, and test data in MySQL database.
4.  Run the application in Studio.

After Studio deploys the application, you can query your OData service using the following query
examples:

### GET Request Example

Request

```curl
curl --location --request GET 'localhost:8081/api/Customers('BLAUS')'
```

Response

```JSON
{
  "@odata.context": "http://localhost:8081/api/$metadata#Customers/$entity",
  "CompanyName": "Blauer See Delikatessen",
  "ContactName": "Hanna Moos",
  "ContactTitle": "Sales Representative",
  "CustomerID": "BLAUS"
}
```

#### GET Request Example With Composite Key

Request

```curl
curl --location --request GET 'localhost:8081/api/Orders(OrderID=10315,ShipName='Island Trading')
```

Response

```JSON
{
  "@odata.context": "http://localhost:8081/api/$metadata#Orders/$entity",
  "Freight": 41.76,
  "OrderDate": "1996-09-26T00:00:00Z",
  "OrderID": "10315",
  "Price": null,
  "Priority": 1,
  "ShipAddress": "Garden house Crowther Way",
  "ShipName": "Island Trading"
}
```

### Create Example

In this example, the client provides the key (or keys) to create a new entity:

POST Customer Request

```curl
curl --location --request POST 'http://localhost:8081/api/Customers' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--data-raw '{
   "CompanyName": "Mulesoft",
   "ContactName": "Customer 123",
   "CustomerID": "NW123"
}'
```

POST Order

```curl
curl --location --request POST 'http://localhost:8081/api/Orders' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--data-raw '{
   "Freight": 32.38,
   "OrderID": 100000,
   "ShipAddress": "Unknown St. 123",
   "ShipName": "Order 100000"
}'
```

### Update Example

OData V4 recommends using PATCH to update entities because it only changes the provided fields:

PATCH

```curl
curl --location --request PATCH 'http://localhost:8081/api/Customers('\''BOTTM'\'')' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--data-raw '{
   "ContactName": "James Bottom"
}'
```

```curl
curl --location --request PATCH 'http://localhost:8081/api/Orders(OrderID=10251,ShipName='\''Victuailles en stock'\'')' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--data-raw '{
   "ShipAddress": "Unknown Av. 1234"
}'
```

OData V4 discourages the usage of PUT to update your entities because PUT replaces the entire entity
with the new one, which can lead to data loss.

PUT

```curl
curl --location --request PUT 'http://localhost:8081/api/Customers('\''LONEP'\'')' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--data-raw '{
   "CompanyName": "New Lonesome Pine Restaurant",
   "ContactName": "Fran C. Wilson",
   "CustomerID": "LONEP"
}'
```

```curl
curl --location --request PUT 'http://localhost:8081/api/Orders(OrderID=11056,ShipName='\''Eastern Connection'\'')' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--data-raw '{
   "Freight": 27.52,
   "OrderDate": "1998-05-28T00:00:00",
   "OrderID": 11056,
   "Priority": 2,
   "ShipAddress": "45 King George",
   "ShipName": "Eastern Connection"
}'
```

### Delete Example

To delete the entity:

DELETE

```curl
curl --location --request DELETE 'http://localhost:8081/api/Customers('\''NW123'\'')'
```

```curl
curl --location --request DELETE 'http://localhost:8081/api/Orders(OrderID=11056,ShipName='\''Eastern Connection'\'')'
```

### Client-Side Pagination in APIkit for OData 4

You can configure your client to request the page using the HTTP query parameters `$skip` and
`$top`.

- The `$top` system query parameter specifies a non-negative integer _n_ that limits the number of
  items returned from a collection.
- The `$skip` system query parameter specifies a non-negative integer _n_ that excludes the first
  _n_ items of the queried collection from the result.

For example:

```curl
curl -X GET √¢¬Ä¬òlocalhost:8081/api/Customers?$skip=1&$top=5√¢¬Ä¬ô
```

Returns five records starting from the second position.

### Server-Side Pagination in APIkit for OData 4

Server-side pagination enables you to control a subset of data requests fetched from a client-side
server by using a fixed `pageSize` parameter. It divides the response data set into discrete pages
to improve data readability. The server defines the page size, which is the maximum number of
records that the server returns in a request.

#### Server-Side Pagination Operation in OData 4

OData defines a `@odata.nextLink` to indicate that a response is only a subset of the requested
collection of entities. As such, `@odata.nextLink` contains a URL that enables the client to
retrieve the next subset of the requested collection. The URL contains a query parameter
`$skiptoken` that indicates the server where the next page starts.

#### Implement Server-Side Pagination in APIkit for OData 4

You can implement the server-side pagination by defining a set of fields with
`<apikit-odata:request-entity-collection-listener>` in the **Responses** tab in Anypoint Studio.

![pageSize set to ${service.orders.pageSize} in the On Entity Collection Request tab](_images/pagesize.png)

After `pageSize` is populated, APIkit creates the XML representation of the previous fields:

```xml
<apikit-odata:request-entity-collection-listener config-ref="odata-metadata-config" path="/Orders" method="GET" >
        <apikit-odata:collection-success-response >
            <apikit-odata:entity-collection-success-response pageSize="${service.orders.pageSize}"/>
        </apikit-odata:collection-success-response>
    <apikit-odata:request-entity-collection-listener>
```

#### Page Size or Token Fields for Server-Side Pagination

`Page size` is a fixed number that represents the maximum number of records the
`request-entity-collection` listener can retrieve for each request. In case you cannot use a numeric
pagination, set a token in the `Token field`, so a developer can generate it in the corresponding
flow. APIkit for OData includes this token value in the `@odata.nextLink` URL with a query parameter
`$skiptoken=<Token_Value>`.

## See Also

- [APIkit for OData v4 Module Reference](apikit-odatav4-extension-reference)
- [APIkit for OData v4 Error Handling Reference](apikit-odatav4-error-handling-reference)
- [Deploy to CloudHub](../../cloudhub/deploying-to-cloudhub)
---

# Implementing a SOAP Interface

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Use Anypoint Studio and APIkit for SOAP to build a Mule integration that calls a remote SOAP service
and exposes it as part of your API surface. The walkthrough uses
[tshirt2.wsdl](_attachments/tshirt2.wsdl) so you map WSDL operations to flows and connect downstream
systems behind the SOAP contract.

Confirm if
[APIkit for SOAP 1.7.0 or 2.0.0](apikit-4-for-soap#choose-the-apikit-for-soap-module-version)
matches your Mule runtime and JDK versions before you scaffold your project.

Start by [creating an APIkit for SOAP project](apikit-4-soap-project-task).

## See Also

- [APIkit for REST Module Reference](apikit-4-xml-reference)
---

# APIkit Overview

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

APIkit scaffolds Mule applications from AsyncAPI, gRPC, GraphQL, OData, RAML, OpenAPI Specification
(OAS), and SOAP contracts, validates inbound traffic against those contracts, and can generate
[MUnit test suites](../../munit/latest/munit-scaffold-test-task). Each protocol has a dedicated
module; routing dispatches requests to the flows that implement your resources. The same general
workflow√¢¬Ä¬îspecify, scaffold, implement, test√¢¬Ä¬îapplies across modules, so skills transfer among SOAP,
REST, GraphQL, and event-driven APIs.

## Supported Web Service Types

- [APIkit for AsyncAPI](apikit-for-asyncapi) supports AsyncAPI APIs.
- [APIkit for gRPC](apikit-for-grpc) supports gRPC APIs.
- [APIkit for GraphQL](apikit-4-for-graphql) supports GraphQL APIs.
- [APIkit for OData V2](apikit-4-for-odatav2) and [APIkit for OData V4](apikit-4-for-odatav4)
  support OData APIs.
- [APIkit for REST](apikit-4-for-rest) supports RAML and OAS APIs.
- [APIkit for SOAP](apikit-4-for-soap) supports SOAP APIs.

## Features

- APIkit generates the structure of a Mule application based on a supported API specification.
- APIkit includes sources and operations that perform complex input validations according to the
  requirements in the API specification.
- The APIkit for REST module includes an API console that enables you to experiment with the API
  project as you develop it.
- Some APIkit modules include extensions for [Anypoint Studio](../../studio/) and
  [Anypoint Code Builder](../../anypoint-code-builder/) for enhancing your editing experience in the
  supported development environments.

## Before You Begin Using APIkit

You must have access to [Anypoint Studio](../../studio/) or
[Anypoint Code Builder](../../anypoint-code-builder/) to scaffold a supported API specification with
APIkit.

## APIkit Versions

APIkit works as a module with Mule runtime version 4.

By default, Studio includes the APIkit for REST and APIkit for SOAP modules. If you need the GraphQL
or OData modules, follow the installation instructions in each section.

## See Also

- [APIkit Compatibility](apikit-compatibility)
- [APIkit for AsyncAPI](apikit-for-asyncapi)
- [APIkit for gRPC](apikit-for-grpc)
- [APIkit for GraphQL](apikit-4-for-graphql)
- [APIkit for OData V2](apikit-4-for-odatav2)
- [APIkit for OData V4](apikit-4-for-odatav4)
- [APIkit for REST](apikit-4-for-rest)
- [APIkit for SOAP](apikit-4-for-soap)
---

# Installing OData V2 Tooling

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Install the APIkit for OData v2 Studio plugin so you can generate OData APIs from RAML types, edit
metadata, and scaffold flows. Add the update site in Anypoint Studio, select the OData extension,
and restart when the installer finishes.

1.  Select **Help** > **Install New Software** > **Add**.

    ![Window for installing new software with the *Add* button highlighted](_images/add-update-site.png)

2.  In **Name**, type `APIkit for ODATA v2 Update Site`.
3.  In **Location**, type the URL of the site: `https://studio.mulesoft.org/s4/apikit-for-odata/`.
4.  Select **Add**.

    ![Window for adding a repository with the *Add* button, with the name the location fields highlighted](_images/add-repository-odata.png)

5.  Select **Anypoint ODATA Plugin** and **Next**.

    ![Window highlighting the Anypoint OData plugin field and the Next button](_images/select-odata-extension.png)

6.  Accept the terms and conditions of the product and select **Finish**.
7.  Restart Studio to complete the installation.

## See Also

- [Building, Implementing, and Testing an OData V2 API](creating-an-odatav2-api-with-apikit)
---

# Installing OData V4 Tooling

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Install the APIkit for OData v4 Studio plugin to author CSDL metadata, scaffold OData v4 services,
and align tooling with your Mule runtime. Register the update site in Anypoint Studio, install the
OData extension, and restart to activate it.

1.  Select **Help** > **Install New Software** and then click **Add**.

    ![Window for installing new software with the *Add* button highlighted](_images/add-update-site.png)

2.  In **Name**, type `APIkit for ODATA v4 Update Site`.
3.  In **Location**, type the URL of the site: `https://studio.mulesoft.org/s4/apikit-for-odata4/`.
4.  Click **Add**.

    ![Window for adding a repository highlighting the *Add* button, with the name and location fields](_images/add-repository-odatav4.png)

5.  Select **Anypoint ODATA Plugin** and then click **Next**.

    ![Window highlighting the Anypoint OData plugin field with the Next button](_images/select-odatav4-extension.png)

6.  Accept the terms and conditions of the product and click **Finish**.
7.  Restart Studio to complete the installation.

## See Also

- [Building, Implementing, and Testing an OData V4 API](creating-an-odatav4-api-with-apikit)
---

# Configuring Behavior During Runtime

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Tune APIkit for REST at run time by tightening validation, shaping HTTP responses, and keeping base
URIs consistent behind proxies. Each linked task covers one concern you can combine in the same
project.

## Tasks

- [Validating Query Parameters and Headers](validate-4-task)
- [Configuring the Response Headers and Status Code](configure-headers4-task)
- [Configure the Service URI](configure-service-uri-task)
---

# Validating Query Parameters and Headers

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Restrict query parameters and headers on APIkit for REST to the names your RAML or OpenAPI
Specification defines. Turn on strict validation in the **APIkit Router** configuration in Studio,
or set the matching attributes on `apikit:config` in XML when you need repeatable deployments.

1.  In Studio, select **APIkit Router**.

    ![APIkit router highlighted](_images/apikit-router-selection.png)

2.  Click **Edit** for your router configuration.

    ![Edit button highlighted in the APIkit router tab](_images/router-config.png)

3.  Select **Query Parameters Strict Validations** to restrict the usage of query parameters to the
    ones defined in your API specification.
4.  Select **Headers Strict Validations** to limit the headers to the ones specified in your API
    specification.

    ![*Query parameters Strict Validations* and *Headers Strict Validations* highlighted in the router configuration tab](_images/enable-verifications.png)

You can also configure **APIkit Router** to validate headers and query parameters by updating the
APIkit configuration in XML:

```xml
<apikit:config  name="api-config"
                api="api.raml"
                outboundHeadersMapName="outboundHeaders"
                httpStatusVarName="httpStatus"
                queryParamsStrictValidation="true"
                headersStrictValidation="true" />
```

## Disable Validation of Query Parameters and Headers

Disable the validation of query parameters and headers to minimize latency.

1.  In Studio, select **APIkit Router**.

    ![APIkit router highlighted](_images/apikit-router-selection.png)

2.  Click **Edit** for your router configuration.

    ![Edit button highlighted in the APIkit router tab](_images/router-config.png)

3.  Select **Disable Validations**.

    ![*Disable validations* option highlighted in the router configuration tab](_images/disable-verifications-apikit.png)

You can also disable the validation of query parameters and headers to minimize latency by updating
the APIkit configuration in XML:

```xml
<apikit:config  name="api-config"
                api="api.raml"
                outboundHeadersMapName="outboundHeaders"
                httpStatusVarName="httpStatus"
                disableValidations="true" />
```

## See Also

- [Configuring the Response Headers and Status Code](configure-headers4-task)
- [Configure the Service URI](configure-service-uri-task)
